name: GavinNewsom.com
subject: We are writing to ask you to contribute $3 to NARAL ahead of the fight to pass a constitutional amendment protecting the right to access an abortion in California.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY NEWSOM FOR CALIFORNIA – GOVERNOR – 2022
body:
We are not just going to roll over in California.

Friends -
No matter what the Supreme Court decides, we are going to make sure women who need an abortion have access in California.
It’s codified in statute, and this November we’re going to pass a constitutional amendment that protects that right further.
One group who will be at the front of that fight is NARAL.
So as we gear up for the big constitutional amendment campaign, they need our help. Because passing an amendment like this is never easy.
So please:
[**Make a $3 contribution to NARAL today and they will put your donation to work helping pass a constitutional amendment this November that protects the right to access an abortion in California.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gcn-em-naral?refcode=em-220616-1-15&refcode2=853_4767699_FSdev5&akid=853%2E4767699%2EFSdev5)
[CONTRIBUTE $3 TO NARAL](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gcn-em-naral?refcode=em-220616-1-15&refcode2=853_4767699_FSdev5&akid=853%2E4767699%2EFSdev5)
We are not just going to roll over in California. We have been preparing for this moment for some time now. But making sure organizations like NARAL have the resources they need is critical to our success.
So thank you in advance for chipping in today.
Gavin Newsom for Governor
[CONTRIBUTE $3 TO NARAL](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gcn-em-naral?refcode=em-220616-1-15&refcode2=853_4767699_FSdev5&akid=853%2E4767699%2EFSdev5)

name: Andy Kim
subject: When we see ourselves in our nation’s story, we see ourselves in our nation
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Andy Kim for Congress
body:
| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/46717544/351474944/1642521363?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84Nzk3MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjNWY2YTY3Ny0wYmYwLWVjMTEtYjQ3YS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=UVfLmqCjrK4joPBgWBOSx5FH8CtqCIM5kclxPsiY490=&emci=0994253a-71ee-ec11-b47a-281878b83d8a&emdi=c5f6a677-0bf0-ec11-b47a-281878b83d8a&ceid=26261389)
---

| | **Today we celebrate Juneteenth.** Juneteenth commemorates the moment the last enslaved people in the United States learned of President Lincoln's Emancipation Proclamation and were freed two years after he issued the proclamation. **It's a celebration of freedom, known as America's second independence day.** America becomes more inclusive when we find a way to include every family's story in our national tapestry — when we show and tell the world that everyone belongs here. We took a significant step toward expanding our nation's story when Juneteenth became a federal holiday last year. _Representation matters._ **When we see ourselves in our nation's story, we see ourselves in our nation.** And every day, we strive for a more equitable America. Too many people I talk to don't know the story of Juneteenth. Thankfully the Smithsonian has some incredible resources available. There's even a section on sharing the significance of Juneteenth with the children in your life. [LEARN MORE ABOUT JUNETEENTH](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/46717545/351474945/923011606?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84Nzk3MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjNWY2YTY3Ny0wYmYwLWVjMTEtYjQ3YS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=UVfLmqCjrK4joPBgWBOSx5FH8CtqCIM5kclxPsiY490=&emci=0994253a-71ee-ec11-b47a-281878b83d8a&emdi=c5f6a677-0bf0-ec11-b47a-281878b83d8a&ceid=26261389) **I hope you are able to take some time today to reflect on our country's story and the power of freedom.** Happy Juneteenth! _Andy_ |
---|---|---

subject: Reminder: please share your thoughts on Medicaid expansion

body:
When it comes to health, Texas is falling behind. Expanding Medicaid just makes sense. Please share your thoughts below:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/therealtexas_upsl?refcode=051622&amount=25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/therealtexas_upsl?refcode=051622&amount=25]
When the Affordable Care Act was passed, Texas was given the opportunity to receive $100 billion in federal funding in exchange for increasing Medicaid eligibility.
Texas Republicans refused this offer.
Health care coverage in Texas is among the worst in the nation. Nearly 20% of Texans are uninsured. Expanding Medicaid could drastically reduce this number.
What do you think? Should action be taken to immediately expand Medicaid and insure more Texans?

name: Team Beto
subject: Juneteenth
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Beto for Texas
body:
[1]Beto O'Rourke

We’re celebrating Juneteenth today and remembering the origins of our
nation's newest federal holiday. It was on this date in 1865 that those
who were enslaved in Galveston were informed of their freedom — more than
two and a half years after the Emancipation Proclamation.

Together, we honor this powerful moment of liberation in our history and
the heroes who devoted their lives toward the abolition of slavery.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=bft-em-20220619-1.XapoSk&refcode=ema20220619-194650&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1001&akid=194650%2E444211%2E4T92zK ]Photo of Beto marching in Juneteenth parade in 2018

Beto joined Opal Lee in Fort Worth yesterday for her Juneteenth Walk for
Freedom.

This day also requires us to reflect on how much more work we have to do
to achieve true Black liberation. The full promise of Juneteenth is not
yet realized as we fail to address a legacy of slavery and segregation
that is still alive in this country. We saw this last summer when Governor
Greg Abbott pursued voter suppression formulated to keep Black Texans from
having their voice heard and vote counted.

The truth is that we still have work to do. When Texas leads the country
in the number of people who cannot see a doctor each year that Abbott has
been governor, and the rate of maternal mortality is three times higher
for Black women than white women in a state that leads the developed world
in the rate of maternal mortality, we have work to do.

When six out of ten Black Texans don’t make a living wage and millions
live below the federal poverty line, we have work to do. When white Texans
are more likely to receive a diploma than Black Texans, we have work to
do.

The fight for true freedom, justice, and equity must go on. So today,
let's celebrate Juneteenth by committing to full justice in Texas and
supporting the ongoing work to end structural racism.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=bft-em-20220619-1.XapoSk&refcode=ema20220619-194650&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1002&akid=194650%2E444211%2E4T92zK ]If
you're with us in this work for full civil rights, civil liberties,
equality, and opportunity, please consider a contribution of any amount
today to power Beto’s campaign to be a governor who is focused on and
fighting for the people of Texas.

Thank you,

Beto for Texas

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://act.betoorourke.com/unsubscribe/bft-unsubscribe/?t=1003&akid=194650%2E444211%2E4T92zK

Pol. Adv. Paid for by Beto for Texas.

| Special Guests will be announced as we get closer to the event. Food and Non-Alcoholic Beverages will be provided.
The location of Imer Campaign HQ will be emailed to you 3 days prior to the event due to security precautions.
Please note that this event will be outside and we ask that you bring a lawn chair to ensure you have seating.
More details will be announced in the coming days so stay tuned to the website and social media for updates about the event.
This event is FREE to attend and we ask that you invite your friends and neighbors to attend as well!
Team Imer can't wait to see you and we are looking forward to watching the results live at HQ with our biggest supporters! Make sure to RSVP today.
P.S. Team Imer recommends and requests that you DO NOT MAIL IN YOUR BALLOT.
We suggest that you drop your ballot in an indoor/monitored drop box either the day before or the day of the election. We also suggest that you vote in person at a local polling place once in-person voting begins.
These precautions help negate the risk of voter fraud and further interference in our elections, an issue we all hold near and dear to our hearts.
Last but not least, we encourage you to research your candidates, follow the money and have all the facts before voting. Uneducated voters have led to the disastrous leadership we are currently under. Make sure that you are educated on your choice before it is too late to change your vote or mind.

---
| Sincerely,

name: Rep. Val Demings
subject: Read Time: 30 seconds
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Val Demings for U
body:
| | |
---
Friend, I’m asking you for $5.
Here’s why: Polls show me neck and neck with Marco Rubio – and, according to The Miami Times, I’m rapidly closing the gap.
I know $5 doesn't sound like much, but that's how we've built the momentum to make our race competitive. When thousands of people like you join our team with even $5, we build a powerful and unstoppable movement.

But the GOP dark-money machine is determined to destroy our momentum and defeat me at any cost, so I’m leaning on this grassroots team again to help flip Florida blue.

Humbly asking for your support

Hey,

I know my team has been reaching out to you a lot lately, but unfortunately - this can’t wait.

**It’s Ted here, and we have extended our fundraising deadline because it’s such a big milestone.**

**_It’s important that we meet our fundraising goals, so I’ve decided to extend the deadline for another 24 hours!_**

[**I’m humbly asking you to chip-in any amount today to help us reach our goal – even $25 helps.**](http://links.buddfornc.com/u/click?_t=13365b87b52649cc8d23e393a66fd89d&_m=5abfec84e99f4100b392d673ae7c9a97&_e=kLw3cVFPqzWe0pcMhk9Wh8iqNMEMZMZxkMEu0Tz6x_xwiIIhtX9o9aA9XQyZXsGGsN6cUF0eWZGaBZFJZOH4tRWzoQnjYVmJNHTI56D0ZufDxGsX0zq2I-M4DayATJSHWQ8RQxvSU0-Q50ZYlm7pPLYbEDs8-IUZ62ZZAnbc57cvAztzxBdEhyAiVNRTB9_LvBdYoUXKEHuliRsHzyUTySibwOZagmKdcxav7lenxgiNNpoENCDkyBSckf9koudpGg-L2Jx6Ey0-q_GFjVI3gegQ0SwbSVqmOEkJa1BPjvw9CclsV7A8jJEKnY4xEyfAXA8KgeNFpajyTDVuaTKsXI-WnlQXfNcYhLQzIcj1NkhTLxIDRAy_IgAfNgC65UYmpxqcYidVOpMSvLmqAd-tPl3ZhG6UXwuhGYFALYHo1J6y0naFCiRt5WZV_VB-hijC_C0SwugwHtQK9BZEVepVX6jtOCMN5DEGl9Be6wG01spGvhJhYtpW28WpG0Cpsc3j) I know it’s asking for a lot, but I promise to be a fighter for you in the Senate. I promise to crush the Liberal Agenda and fight for our North Carolina values because Cheri Beasley sure wouldn’t.

I know it seems like we keep saying it, but these are **_REAL_** goals and **_REAL_** deadlines. We are headed to a general election in just a few months that will determine the fate of North Carolina and of the Senate!

[**So please, if you can spare anything, please donate to ensure we defeat the Left this November.**](http://links.buddfornc.com/u/click?_t=13365b87b52649cc8d23e393a66fd89d&_m=5abfec84e99f4100b392d673ae7c9a97&_e=kLw3cVFPqzWe0pcMhk9Wh8iqNMEMZMZxkMEu0Tz6x_xwiIIhtX9o9aA9XQyZXsGGsN6cUF0eWZGaBZFJZOH4tRWzoQnjYVmJNHTI56D0ZufDxGsX0zq2I-M4DayATJSHWQ8RQxvSU0-Q50ZYlm7pPLYbEDs8-IUZ62ZZAnbc57cvAztzxBdEhyAiVNRTB9_LvBdYoUXKEHuliRsHzyUTySibwOZagmKdcxav7lenxgiNNpoENCDkyBSckf9koudpzCKh-d7P54vvIxBI3diVXxmoLioSTkYriPkcCSLDJpa6-cT5sGOtFJ4kqNepwVBFhNBZZH6a4iNbBjEf2Lp9PckcYOina1nGsWeHCeh5lsWdEnIhhbmRmR8pyDZhsC7VsBrNIfFafjjO3nEHGckId3DccrPtCzt2K6ctJguxumpWeU_nYTqt1Bs88Vne2jZv-Ugl5bkeAojwsuHgCrfRBSrXpk4nTqVMyzV50d5ajy0%3D) [**DONATE $25 NOW >>**](http://links.buddfornc.com/u/click?_t=13365b87b52649cc8d23e393a66fd89d&_m=5abfec84e99f4100b392d673ae7c9a97&_e=kLw3cVFPqzWe0pcMhk9Wh8iqNMEMZMZxkMEu0Tz6x_xwiIIhtX9o9aA9XQyZXsGGsN6cUF0eWZGaBZFJZOH4tRWzoQnjYVmJNHTI56D0ZufDxGsX0zq2I-M4DayATJSHWQ8RQxvSU0-Q50ZYlm7pPLYbEDs8-IUZ62ZZAnbc57cvAztzxBdEhyAiVNRTB9_LvBdYoUXKEHuliRsHzyUTySibwOZagmKdcxav7lenxgiNNpoENCDkyBSckf9koudpGg-L2Jx6Ey0-q_GFjVI3gegQ0SwbSVqmOEkJa1BPjvzEOrwa6QcihxiV6Rp7wA6Zvqe7E-o_IfwsoCnptMnY3OLj4yRhzvV6THaNmXr7kn_5iMW0DrMecB6IOhlnChrcpSnN3I82cPBI3dKbLS0zU2bC83QH2jI0IgHluQvOJmtVPYBbvq4XryokGYVOCX6IDp2qBb3SV04essE75IitvfVIS6-xRo7Pr_tMoPi2gVh5uYI-bbkCaSY61o4PJXQS)

In Liberty,

Ted Budd

clean_body: Humbly asking for your support | Hey, I know my team has been reaching out to you a lot lately, but unfortunately - this can’t wait. It’s Ted here, and we have extended our fundraising deadline because it’s such a big milestone. It’s important that we meet our fundraising goals, so I’ve decided to extend the deadline for another 24 hours! | I know it’s asking for a lot, but I promise to be a fighter for you in the Senate. I promise to crush the Liberal Agenda and fight for our North Carolina values because Cheri Beasley sure wouldn’t. I know it seems like we keep saying it, but these are REAL goals and REAL deadlines. We are headed to a general election in just a few months that will determine the fate of North Carolina and of the Senate! Thanks in advance for your support. I’m forever grateful. In Liberty, Ted Budd | Ted Budd is a father, small business owner, and conservative leader running for Senate in North Carolina. He is the ONLY candidate who is endorsed by President Trump and the only candidate who will fight for the America First Agenda in Washington. To support our campaign, please click below to make a donation. | | | ---|---|--- Paid for by Ted Budd for Senate Copyright © 2021 Ted Budd for Senate, All rights reserved.

name: Andrew P. McIndoe
subject: The state of America’s energy independence
body:
http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/p/vsB_HILh1N

http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/p/osB_HILh1N

Dear
Fellow Conservative,
Gas
prices have risen dramatically since the Biden administration
took office, and I'm sure you've felt the pinch at the pump just
like the rest of your fellow Americans.
There's
no doubt in my mind that our nation's citizens are in need of
relief. But instead, President Biden has throttled U.S. energy
production. And he has trapped much of our nation's vast energy
reserves behind a wall of bureaucratic red tape.
That's
why I'm sending you this email today.
I'd like
to hear how the Biden administration's energy policies have
affected you and your family.

Would you
be willing to share your thoughts in this brief 8 question
survey?
By doing
so, you will help The Heritage Foundation understand what people
like you think-and use your answers to fight back against the
Biden administration's radical anti-energy agenda.
The
survey should only take a few minutes of your time, but your
opinion is incredibly valuable. You can get started right now
using this secure link:
https://secured.heritage.org/energy-independence-survey/
Thank you
so much for your time. I am truly grateful.
Best,
Andrew P.
McIndoe
VP,
Development

The
Heritage Foundation 214 Massachusetts Avenue NE Washington
D.C. 20002

clean_body: Dear Fellow Conservative, Gas prices have risen dramatically since the Biden administration took office, and I'm sure you've felt the pinch at the pump just like the rest of your fellow Americans. There's no doubt in my mind that our nation's citizens are in need of relief. But instead, President Biden has throttled U.S. energy production. And he has trapped much of our nation's vast energy reserves behind a wall of bureaucratic red tape. That's why I'm sending you this email today. I'd like to hear how the Biden administration's energy policies have affected you and your family. Would you be willing to share your thoughts in this brief 8 question survey? By doing so, you will help The Heritage Foundation understand what people like you think-and use your answers to fight back against the Biden administration's radical anti-energy agenda. The survey should only take a few minutes of your time, but your opinion is incredibly valuable. You can get started right now using this secure link: Thank you so much for your time. I am truly grateful. Best, Andrew P. ** _A Note From Nikki:_** Growing up, smart, strong, and unconventional women were discouraged. When I ran for office, the South Carolina legislature was dominated by a Good Old Boys' Club and my opponent thought women shouldn’t be in politics. People thought I was too brown, too female, too young, too conservative, too principled. I said: Too bad. I was inspired by great women who broke barriers and defied expectations, women like Margaret Thatcher and Jeane Kirkpatrick. That’s why I wrote this book. "If You Want Something Done..." is set to be released on October 4. **[Pre-order your signed copy today to have it shipped to you once it launches in clean_body: | | | Friend, America’s cities are in disarray. Murder, arson, and theft rates are skyrocketing in cities across the country. Democrats’ soft-on-crime policies are failing our nation’s cities, and we need your help to put an end to them. Let’s work together to get a wave of freedom-loving conservatives elected. Thank you, Stand For America PAC | A Note From Nikki: Growing up, smart, strong, and unconventional women were discouraged. When I ran for office, the South Carolina legislature was dominated by a Good Old Boys' Club and my opponent thought women shouldn’t be in politics. People thought I was too brown, too female, too young, too conservative, too principled. I said: Too bad. I was inspired by great women who broke barriers and defied expectations, women like Margaret Thatcher and Jeane Kirkpatrick. That’s why I wrote this book. "If You Want Something Done..." is set to be released on October 4. Dear Congresswoman Cheney, I might not agree with you on much, but we both know one thing to be true: Trump-loving MAGA Republicans are a grave threat to our democracy. That’s why I’m so thankful for your hard work on the Jan. 6th Commission. You’ve bucked the corrupt Trump wing of your party to search for the TRUTH – and I couldn’t be more grateful! = | = Congresswoman Cheney, I know you’ve faced attacks from your party before. When you called out Donald Trump for inciting the deadly Jan. 6th insurrection, his cronies in Congress responded by censuring your voice. It was pathetic! I know these attacks have made your search for the truth impossibly hard... but let me assure you, Congresswoman, that good Americans are with you! = = Thank you for being on the right side of history. Watching them grow and become more of themselves each day challenges me to fight for the world I want them to grow up in. **But, they can’t sit still**. [__](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/46718143/351478508/1668440331?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODMxNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNWU5YmU4Mi0xN2YwLWVjMTEtYjQ3YS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=IfgY-S-NIXjuFm2arJAa-9qAJxwhaE2GHMmJkj0-Kgg=&emci=d4e9be82-17f0-ec11-b47a-281878b83d8a&emdi=d5e9be82-17f0-ec11-b47a-281878b83d8a&ceid=28008880) On the campaign trail, so much goes on in the day-to-day that things can naturally fall through the cracks. But at the end of a long day, getting a snack with the kids or sharing the latest news with Caroline keeps me focused on the mission. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/46718144/351478509/1668440331?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODMxNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNWU5YmU4Mi0xN2YwLWVjMTEtYjQ3YS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=IfgY-S-NIXjuFm2arJAa-9qAJxwhaE2GHMmJkj0-Kgg=&emci=d4e9be82-17f0-ec11-b47a-281878b83d8a&emdi=d5e9be82-17f0-ec11-b47a-281878b83d8a&ceid=28008880) Also, Adeline would be upset if I didn’t mention her other sibling, Fireball. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/46718145/351478510/1668440331?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODMxNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNWU5YmU4Mi0xN2YwLWVjMTEtYjQ3YS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=IfgY-S-NIXjuFm2arJAa-9qAJxwhaE2GHMmJkj0-Kgg=&emci=d4e9be82-17f0-ec11-b47a-281878b83d8a&emdi=d5e9be82-17f0-ec11-b47a-281878b83d8a&ceid=28008880) So this Father’s Day, I want to quickly thank you for your support. Unfortunately, Joe Biden and the rest of the Washington SWAMP are working overtime to undo everything my administration accomplished. Mark my words, America will be put FIRST again, but only with the help of my most loyal supporters. name: LBR
subject: Today, we recommit ourselves to continuing down the long road toward justice
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Lisa Blunt Rochester for Congress Lisa Blunt Rochester for Congress
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/46718291/351479243/1893886755?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85Mjg3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjNzgyNmI3Mi0xYWYwLWVjMTEtYjQ3YS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Wlm9wjJvW4gejUwEn1IQ4iFSmnx_tE-IxN9NujrBw8c=&emci=83adc80b-13f0-ec11-b47a-281878b83d8a&emdi=c7826b72-1af0-ec11-b47a-281878b83d8a&ceid=11913588) [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/46718292/351479244/1893886755?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85Mjg3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjNzgyNmI3Mi0xYWYwLWVjMTEtYjQ3YS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Wlm9wjJvW4gejUwEn1IQ4iFSmnx_tE-IxN9NujrBw8c=&emci=83adc80b-13f0-ec11-b47a-281878b83d8a&emdi=c7826b72-1af0-ec11-b47a-281878b83d8a&ceid=11913588) Friend, this is the second year in which we've observed Juneteenth as a federal holiday. I'm incredibly proud of all the activists, like Opal Lee, who campaigned across the country to make Juneteenth a holiday, and lawmakers who helped move the needle to make Juneteenth a holiday.

When I reflect on this holiday, I am reminded of the saying “truth crushed to earth shall rise.” On Juneteenth, we observe the end of slavery in the United States -- when the people of Galveston, Texas were notified of their freedom a full two years after the signing of the Emancipation Proclamation. Truth be told, slavery would persist in our own state of Delaware and in Kentucky until December 1865, when the 13th Amendment took on the force of law.

Today, as we recommit ourselves to continuing down the long road toward justice, we should all take time to remember those who came before us. Our ancestors who were packed into ships and brought against their will to a new continent; the people held as chattel who knew no life beyond a plantation; freedom fighters like Dr. King, my late friend John Lewis, and Delawarean Mary Ann Shadd Cary.

**The best way to honor all of those who came before us is to pick up their mantle and continue to bend the long arc of the moral universe toward justice.**

So today, I’m asking you to join me in recommitting to the fight for a more equitable world. Whether it’s joining your friends and neighbors to fix an injustice in your community, volunteering your time to educate yourself and the next generation, or simply acknowledging the residual effects of slavery in our day-to-day lives, anything can make a difference in our continued march toward freedom. Thank you,

Congresswoman Lisa Blunt Rochester ** ** ** ** ** ** ** ** ** ** Paid for by Lisa Blunt Rochester for Congress Lisa Blunt Rochester for Congress
PO BOX 9767
Wilmington, DE 19810
United States If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please [unsubscribe](https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/_6DeEby01kOvxTWj8qzW6A2?unsubscribedata=rwCiyoTuUt4EkHWZuS1Rzx48CMEvoR6OaXEqyp1jku1H90WPhYD674ONja1YkajqdS%2F0DQLpD2w%2Btdc8MM1vcb%2FvVm8QtM3sBW5vIyOijw%2FkQ%2FxZH0RHHo51zT8XHwh8iJID8RAw6LN2NZqzttKQIDRQsAnzEFALtUM5ixzU0BB7hqWBfvE9GFyslSiQ4bXTvuHXVOhBHdvlcqNpVmlbr%2FfXhChE9%2BNM2QknXqVZWdG8Md23L8EEKmrTV042rQ%2F1gDJMY6YF5YzNX%2BtZdPaWcfZThro4D3fvR5VOlxpe2MFLul4vhdG3Pg7Ybzhu2NoGHAXJ1euLP3Bm1T5pQxUIJhaqFucVyPR7%2BYJbrZwfk64v0aKROrpwakXOzr1MtMLF65PGactL9ist8PPv1owTAZG9aKLzlYzsGNVzxLAu26fXCkUAdddjpMImVpuGZ01k). ** **

clean_body: Friend, this is the second year in which we've observed Juneteenth as a federal holiday. I'm incredibly proud of all the activists, like Opal Lee, who campaigned across the country to make Juneteenth a holiday, and lawmakers who helped move the needle to make Juneteenth a holiday. When I reflect on this holiday, I am reminded of the saying “truth crushed to earth shall rise.” On Juneteenth, we observe the end of slavery in the United States -- when the people of Galveston, Texas were notified of their freedom a full two years after the signing of the Emancipation Proclamation. Truth be told, slavery would persist in our own state of Delaware and in Kentucky until December 1865, when the 13th Amendment took on the force of law. Today, as we recommit ourselves to continuing down the long road toward justice, we should all take time to remember those who came before us. Our ancestors who were packed into ships and brought against their will to a new continent; the people held as chattel who knew no life beyond a plantation; freedom fighters like Dr. King, my late friend John Lewis, and Delawarean Mary Ann Shadd Cary. The best way to honor all of those who came before us is to pick up their mantle and continue to bend the long arc of the moral universe toward justice. So today, I’m asking you to join me in recommitting to the fight for a more equitable world. Whether it’s joining your friends and neighbors to fix an injustice in your community, volunteering your time to educate yourself and the next generation, or simply acknowledging the residual effects of slavery in our day-to-day lives, anything can make a difference in our continued march toward freedom. name: Peter Welch
subject: How I got my start in Vermont
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY WELCH FOR VERMONT
body:
Vermonters taught me how to get things done on Bridge Street, and I haven’t forgotten those lessons.

# [](https://act.welchforvermont.com/go/144?t=2&akid=552%2E61531%2EHQF3pP)
Team,
I first moved to Vermont to work as a lawyer on Bridge Street in White River Junction. And when I wasn’t in the office, I found myself spending as much time possible exploring the natural wonders I had right outside my door.
It was fulfilling work, fighting for the community. And I knew my friends who opted to work on Wall Street in New York or K Street in Washington weren’t able to step outside their homes and onto a trail for running, hiking, or mountain biking.
Bridge Street is where Vermont became my home, and it’s where I got to know the values of Vermonters: self-reliance, mutual respect, and cooperation built on trust.
Right now, it’s awfully clear that the Senate could use some more of Vermont’s values to help move forward. But first, we must do everything we can to actually win here—and with Mitch McConnell targeting our race, it’s an All Hands On Deck moment to keep this seat blue.
**[This is a critical race, and we need this team to help us get it done. If you can, chip in $5 right away.](https://act.welchforvermont.com/go/3018?t=3&akid=552%2E61531%2EHQF3pP)**
_If you’ve saved payment info with ActBlue Express, your donation will process automatically:_

---
| [$3](https://act.welchforvermont.com/go/3018?t=17&amount=3&akid=552%2E61531%2EHQF3pP)
---
| [$15](https://act.welchforvermont.com/go/3018?t=19&amount=15&akid=552%2E61531%2EHQF3pP)
---
| [$28](https://act.welchforvermont.com/go/3018?t=21&amount=28&akid=552%2E61531%2EHQF3pP)
---
| [$50](https://act.welchforvermont.com/go/3018?t=23&amount=50&akid=552%2E61531%2EHQF3pP)
---
| [$100](https://act.welchforvermont.com/go/3018?t=13&amount=100&akid=552%2E61531%2EHQF3pP)
---
| [OTHER](https://act.welchforvermont.com/go/3018?t=15&akid=552%2E61531%2EHQF3pP)
---

---
Bridge Street is where I got my introduction to how Vermonters get things done: by focusing on solutions, not who gets the credit. It’s how I’ve been able to work with anyone to find success for our communities, and I’m proud of the work we’ve already accomplished in Congress.
There’s so much more we can accomplish for the people of Vermont and for working families nationwide. I’m fighting for quality and affordable child care, paid family leave, a Green New Deal, Medicare for All, lower prescription drug costs, protecting the right to abortion, strengthening voting rights, and so much more. We’ve had some victories, but I’ll continue fighting to deliver results on your behalf.
But if Mitch McConnell and the Senate Republicans regain the majority, they will continue their assault on individual rights and our democracy. We can’t let that happen.
[**With our numbers going public at the end of the month, we have to have a strong showing. Can you chip in $5 to power this team to victory?**](https://act.welchforvermont.com/go/3018?t=8&akid=552%2E61531%2EHQF3pP)
Thanks so much,
[](https://act.welchforvermont.com/go/3018?t=9&akid=552%2E61531%2EHQF3pP)
Peter

---

name: Deb McGrath
subject: My thoughts on Juneteenth:
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY DEB MCGRATH FOR CONGRESS
body:
None of us are free until all of us are free.

| Hello Peter,

Juneteenth marks the day some of the last enslaved Americans received word of emancipation - months after the Union won the Civil War and more than two years after President Lincoln issued the Emancipation Proclamation.

After Major General Gordon Granger marched into Galveston, Texas, 157 years ago today to inform the last Confederate hold-outs that the War was over, slavery came to an end in our country.

Juneteenth is a long-overdue recognition of Black Americans’ contributions to this country. But we have more work left to do to reckon with our history.

Although slavery came to an end, our nation is still working to end its legacy of inequality, racism, and injustice. Right now, new voter suppression legislation across the country specifically targets Black communities. Our government was designed to be by and for the people, and our leaders have a duty to ensure that every one of us is able to participate in it.

Peter, until we confront those evils and take concrete steps to solve them, including by passing legislation securing voting rights, we cannot claim to be a nation where all of us are truly free.

We’ve come a long way - that Juneteenth is celebrated as a federal holiday just goes to show the strides we’ve made in the struggle for equality and justice. Today is a reminder that even when change seems impossible, it can happen when enough of us join together to create it. This can always be a more perfect union.

\- Deb | |
---
| PAID FOR BY DEB MCGRATH FOR CONGRESS
---

[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/46715233/351460249/459488397?refcode=jas_headerlogo&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODg4MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlNjdjNTBhMi1mOWVmLWVjMTEtYjQ3YS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=zAdDE8Ccky4QTTmqygkZASvHWNVApobH2l12baTVojM=)

| | Peter, In January 1863, during the middle of the Civil War, President Lincoln issued the Emancipation Proclamation. However, it wasn’t until June 19th two full years later, that slavery officially ended, when federal troops stormed Galveston, Texas. Last year, President Biden declared Juneteenth as a federal holiday – a significant step to nationally recognizing the significance of this day 157 years ago. And while this calls for celebration, it’s also a moment for reflection and commitment to action. We aren’t a free country until we’re all free – and the fight for freedom, equality, and racial justice continues. Institutional racism still exists. Black Americans experience disparities when it comes to healthcare, education, housing, the criminal justice system, and more. Today – and every day – I remain committed to showing up in the fight for racial justice and will continue to do all within my power to repair the incalculable harms of racism and racist violence. Happy Juneteenth, Peter. In solidarity, Jasmine
---

name: Victoria Virasingh
subject: Father's Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Victoria for Congress
body:
[https://als-totem.s3.amazonaws.com/core/spacer.gif] https://secure.actblue.com/donate/vv-mnc-email?refcode=fathersday_LOGO [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/vv-mnc-email?refcode=fathersday_LOGO] [https://als-totem.s3.amazonaws.com/core/spacer.gif]
Happy Father’s Day to all the father figures in Virginia's 8th and beyond. I continue to be grateful for the sacrifices that my father has made for me and my mother. All my life, he supported my interests and goals, from coaching me in soccer as a kid and driving me to all my school activities to putting up 'Vote Victoria' yard signs all across the district.
I would not be where I am today without my parent's support, and I hope each and every one of you had a wonderful day with your father and father figures.
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/victoria-for-congress?refcode=fathersday [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/victoria-for-congress?refcode=fathersday]
Your support means everything!
Victoria
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/vv-mnc-email?refcode=fathersday_headshot [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/vv-mnc-email?refcode=fathersday_headshot]

If you've stored payment info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution instantly:
$10 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/victoria-for-congress?express_lane=true&amount=10&refcode=fathersdaya_10] $25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/victoria-for-congress?express_lane=true&amount=25&refcode=fathersdaya_25] $50 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/victoria-for-congress?express_lane=true&amount=50&refcode=fathersdaya_50] $100 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/victoria-for-congress?express_lane=true&amount=100&refcode=fathersdaya_100]
Victoria Virasingh is the daughter of immigrants. She grew up housing insecure and watched her working parents struggle to pull their family out of poverty. But today, even those opportunities to work hard and live the American dream are becoming less and less attainable.
That's why Victoria is running for Congress in Virginia's 8th District -- to create equitable opportunities for those who have been left behind, to expand access for the store owner to the refugee, to support our struggling elders and students, and to lift up working families.
Victoria is a fearless progressive champion who will fight for bold policies to improve the daily lives of Virginians, and she's bringing the fresh new leadership that we need to move our state forward.
Pitch in to help elect Victoria Virasingh as the next voice of VA-08 >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/vv-mnc-email?refcode=fathersdayb]

🛑 **STOP** the GOP from filling state courts with judges who they believe will rubber-stamp their gerrymandering schemes.
✅ **SUPPORT** candidates who will defend our democracy and ensure, as President Obama said, that “the rules of the road are fair” for future elections.
**We have a plan – but we can’t execute it without your help. So please, will you donate whatever you can to help us invest in the fight to end gerrymandering across the country?**

If we don’t spend the next several months raising the necessary funds ... we could be caught off guard." – President Barack Obama = We couldn’t agree more, folks. That’s why we’re committed to continuing our fight against gerrymandering by investing in target races where we’ll work to: ❌ DEFEAT Republicans who seek to undermine our elections with map manipulation and voter suppression. 🛑 STOP the GOP from filling state courts with judges who they believe will rubber-stamp their gerrymandering schemes. ✅ SUPPORT candidates who will defend our democracy and ensure, as President Obama said, that “the rules of the road are fair” for future elections. We have a plan – but we can’t execute it without your help. So please, will you donate whatever you can to help us invest in the fight to end gerrymandering across the country? name: Fight for Progress
subject: Celebrate Juneteenth, donate to the NAACP
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fight for Progress PAC and not authorized by any candidate or any candidate's committee
body:
Today marks the 157th anniversary of Juneteenth

|
[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/46716030/351464036/-1929294314?refcode=BLU-FFP-20220619-1-EM&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNS8xLzc3NDA2IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjg3NTU5ZDQyLWZkZWYtZWMxMS1iNDdhLTI4MTg3OGI4M2Q4YSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=SYXYF0TJ2PYo5gGxyrOhmBDNhGAALeeV11PTx28naiA=)
---
| Peter,

Today marks the 157th anniversary of Juneteenth when Black enslaved persons officially gained freedom in Galveston, Texas, two years after the Emancipation Proclamation. And though this marks a moment of appreciation for progress, it’s also a stark reminder of the work that’s left to be done.

The sheer number of present inequities across the United States reminds us that we cannot stop fighting for change and justice. To this day, Black men are five times more likely to be stopped without just cause than a white person, Black women are three times more likely to die from pregnancy-related causes, and Black applicants receive an 80% higher mortgage denial rate than white applicants. The list goes on and on.

[_**We cannot tolerate a nation of such disparity. Donate today to the NAACP, and help fight racial inequality in all forms for a fairer tomorrow.**_](https://click.everyaction.com/k/46716031/351464037/-1929294314?refcode=BLU-FFP-20220619-1-EM&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNS8xLzc3NDA2IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjg3NTU5ZDQyLWZkZWYtZWMxMS1iNDdhLTI4MTg3OGI4M2Q4YSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=SYXYF0TJ2PYo5gGxyrOhmBDNhGAALeeV11PTx28naiA=)

And 111 people are shot and killed while 210 survive traumatic gunshot injuries.1 But the far-right continues to allow the NRA and pro-gun lobbyists to stand in the way of common-sense gun reform that will save lives – including passing an assault weapons ban. As one of just three Democratic physicians serving in Congress, Dr. Raul Ruiz knows that in order to fight back against the gun lobby, we must call gun violence what it is: a public health crisis. That’s exactly why Dr. Ruiz and other House Democrats are fighting nonstop to pass an assault weapons ban in Congress to save lives and make our communities safer. With President Biden and Vice President Harris in the White House, we have a clear path forward. But we can't get complacent. name: Elissa Slotkin
subject: When you grow up with a strict dad
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Elissa Slotkin for Congress
body:
Elissa Slotkin for Congresssecure.actblue.com/donate/slotkinq22022?refcode=email20220619&amount=28 [secure.actblue.com/donate/slotkinq22022?refcode=email20220619&amount=28]
Team,
This Father’s Day, I wanted to celebrate my dad, who, like so many of his generation, was pretty strict growing up. While he has most definitely mellowed with age (and with his grandkids), he raised us kids the way he was raised — with consistent discipline. To set the scene, here’s a picture I found recently, circa 1982, from when I was a kid.
secure.actblue.com/donate/slotkinq22022?refcode=email20220619&amount=28 [secure.actblue.com/donate/slotkinq22022?refcode=email20220619&amount=28]
That’s my brother and some friends with my dad on our farm, sitting in our old Jeep. My dad is one of those fix-it guys who can get a 4-wheeler working and birth a calf, and he ran quite a tight ship when I was a kid.
This spring, I made the decision to move to Lansing after Michigan’s district lines were redrawn, and most of my current district was looped in with the new 7th. However, this new district doesn’t contain the portion of Oakland County I currently represent, including the family farm in Holly.
But don’t worry, the farm isn’t going anywhere. In fact, my dad has acquired a whole new enthusiasm and energy for fixing the place up, keeping it in tip-top shape, and keeping a few local kids from Holly High School busy as they help him with special projects.
Today, we’re all together at the farm celebrating my dad. He’s doing what he does best, running the grill like a drill sergeant, grilling up hot dogs and salami (his speciality), and watching his family enjoy his beloved farm as he quietly smokes a cigar.
secure.actblue.com/donate/slotkinq22022?refcode=email20220619&amount=28 [secure.actblue.com/donate/slotkinq22022?refcode=email20220619&amount=28] secure.actblue.com/donate/slotkinq22022?refcode=email20220619&amount=28 [secure.actblue.com/donate/slotkinq22022?refcode=email20220619&amount=28]
To my dad, and all the dads out there, happy Father’s Day.
CONTRIBUTE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/slotkinq22022?refcode=email20220619&amount=28]
Elissa

name: Pramila Jayapal
subject: Juneteenth in 2022
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Pramila for Congress
body:
Today is an important day in our country's history.
#
Peter,
Juneteenth is an important day in America — it’s a stark reminder of our history. There’s been hard-fought progress towards justice and there’s so much work yet unfinished.
On this day 157 years ago, slavery was finally ended in America. It was on June 19th, 1865 that Union soldiers landed at Galveston, Texas with news that enslaved people were now free.
I’m proud that our Democratic majorities and President Biden made Juneteenth a federal holiday last year. **But we can’t just move on.** Congress still has an enormous amount of work ahead — work to make equality real and address nearly a century of oppressive Jim Crow laws in America.
I’ve spoken about these issues and I discussed them with my supporters on this day last year. But these pressing issues aren’t going away and we need to continue thinking about them and fighting for transformative changes in our society. Here are some of the things I’m continuing to think about today:
**America’s for-profit health care system leaves Black Americans much more likely to be uninsured than white Americans.** Even when seen by a doctor, our Black brothers, sisters, and siblings are less likely to get the care they need. Black women are more likely to die during childbirth, have nearly three times the odds of being hospitalized as white Americans, and are nearly four times more likely to die from COVID-19. Every moment that we refuse to guarantee health care, too many Black Americans are suffering.
**America’s economy is deeply, deeply unequal.** White families have 10 times more wealth than Black families — a racial wealth gap that has gotten worse, not better. This is because we live in a society and an economy that simply does not address longstanding inequities, deprivation of opportunity, or a lack of investment.
**America’s housing system is based on devastating generational impacts that used exclusionary redlining and financial practices to shut out people of color and fuel racial injustice.** This — combined with the lack of economic justice — has caused the housing crisis for Black Americans, who now make up more than 40% of those experiencing homelessness.
**The neighborhoods that many Black Americans live in are perpetually flooded with toxic dump sites and lack of access to clean air, safe drinking water, and public lands.** They are far more likely to be situated next to highways and polluted areas that only worsen the health conditions that exist because of lack of health care.
**America’s education system institutionalizes discrimination to the extent that the schoolhouse to jailhouse pipeline begins as early as kindergarten.** Black students are treated differently because of the color of their skin: disciplined, suspended, and expelled for behavior that elicits no response for white students.
**Our justice system is patently unjust.** At a time when we incarcerate more of our citizens than any other country, Black Americans are being imprisoned at more than five times the rate of white Americans. This is in part due to a war on drugs that was actually a war on Black Americans, locking them up and locking them out of opportunity.
You see, _every single system is stacked on top of another_ — all discriminatory and intertwined, threads of injustice that come together to hold people back. That’s why I am working on and fighting for immediate and urgent legislative solutions to address each of these institutionalized disparities — like Medicare for All, a minimum wage of at least $15 an hour, the PRO Act, the Housing is a Human Right Act, the College for All Act, a Green New Deal, and so much more.
**I didn’t come to Congress to fight for incremental change. I came here to set things straight and to fight for what matters and what we deserve.** This Juneteenth and every single day, I pledge to you that I will stand up to push for real change, in solidarity with the Black community and with all of us who believe in a better world — a world that only we can shape with our own hands and our own will.
Thank you for doing this work with me: with urgency, with generosity, with the belief that we can change things if we work hard enough and urgently enough.
In solidarity,
Pramila

In solidarity, Pramila | | Email us: info@pramilaforcongress.com

Jesse Jackson. I could not have asked for a better role model growing up, one who continues to inspire me today. He taught me how to live a life dedicated to honor, courage, and service to others—lessons I have worked to pass on to my own children. **Happy Father’s Day!** Today also marks the second time we celebrate Juneteenth as a federal holiday, but its history stretches back more than 150 years. The first Juneteenth was on June 19, 1866 in Texas, the place where slavery in America had met its final end one year earlier. The liberated Black men and women who commemorated the first anniversary of their liberation treated it as a day of rest and refreshment, a celebration of the joy of that true freedom so long denied to them, their families, and untold millions. The fact that Juneteenth has been made a federal holiday at long last demonstrates our nation’s progress toward making good on the promise made nearly 250 years ago that the United States would recognize everyone’s right to life, liberty and the pursuit of happiness. But it also reminds us of how hard we have fought to get here, and how much further we must go. **Happy Juneteenth to all. Let us celebrate in the spirit of the first Juneteeth and enjoy this well earned day of rest together, so that we may rise refreshed tomorrow to carry on the fight for justice.** Jonathan _If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process immediately._ | | **[DONATE $10 NOW »](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/46718630/351481572/1912246793?amount=10&express_lane=true&refcode=JJ_EM_EN_WL_220619_1_1&refcode2=2daddbbd-20f0-ec11-b47a-281878b83d8a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTgzOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyZGFkZGJiZC0yMGYwLWVjMTEtYjQ3YS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=B41dIkCrku15DxSSunwNEnAH3mTG0uC2g3WbDzK-mac=)**

name: Jon Kaiman
subject: Happy Juneteenth
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jon Kaiman for Congress
body:
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/FdZI0skcg5BHNUpL4UFB39TRtQshvoL_mvqp6v0YPGQ=/-C2KLzZeuUt1GxM6Wgh81Qi0x3o6mjF5toJvEdLJzb8=)
Peter,
157 years ago today, the last known slaves in Galveston, Texas were informed that they were emancipated, a historic moment where our nation started to address our legacy of injustice and the inhuman treatment of African Americans.
Today, we celebrate freedom and recognize the long journey ahead. While the American dream of self determination, pursuit of happiness, and equal treatment under the law is still not fully felt by all, we continue to advance forward.
Let us recognize that this holiday was gained by those who fought to ensure we remember all our history. Let us mourn those who fought and gave their lives for freedom and justice for all.
Let us celebrate and recommit ourselves to the American promise -- for all.
Happy Juneteenth!
\- Jon Kaiman

Paid for by Jon Kaiman for Congress
---
Jon Kaiman for Congress
P.O. Box 222061
Great Neck NY 11022 United States

clean_body: Virginia is going to be one of the battlegrounds that may decide control over the chamber. | | | Derek, we need to talk about the House of Representatives. Protecting our Senate majority is important. But the impact of keeping or expanding that majority will be muted if Republicans turn around and retake the House. And Virginia is going to be one of the battlegrounds that may decide control over the chamber. = Elaine Luria, Bobby Scott, Donald McEachin, Abigail Spanberger, Don Beyer, Jennifer Wexton, Gerry Connolly. Our hard work in recent election cycles has made it hard to fit all of the names of our commonwealth’s Democratic incumbent house members into one email. But each one is a fantastic representative for their district, and a vote for progress on our most important issues. Last year, every one of these members voted for the Women’s Health Protection Act, which would codify into law the right to an abortion, and nullify whatever action the Supreme Court may be getting ready to take on Roe v. Wade. Hello,
Fox “News” just published its first hit piece on me.
Apparently, never having held elected office, _I_ am somehow responsible for inflation and gas prices... What a laugh.
The “reporter” reached out for comment, but they get the same answer as Russian TV: I don’t participate in the production of propaganda.
The attacks from Chuck Grassley, Mitch McConnell, and the Fox ‘Entertainment’ News propaganda machine have begun. They’re going to spin lie after lie from now until November – anything to try to stop me from beating Grassley this November and turning Iowa blue.
They’re scared. Chuck Grassley has never faced an opponent like me before. A retired military guy with rural roots who is outraising him because of grassroots donors like you? Grassley’s in trouble. And the polls confirm it.
**We can win, but we have to fight back _now_ – and we have to fight back hard. Our fundraising has really slowed down recently, and we’re now dangerously short of our $75,000 goal. name: Yadira Caraveo
subject: Today is Juneteenth
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY CARAVEO FOR CONGRESS
body:
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/46719143/351483521/1132414120?refcode=em_msg_2022.06.19_b1_yc_all&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjUwOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjMGNmZDAyNy0yNGYwLWVjMTEtYjQ3YS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=3Cp4QHgCMjygxCGWDc98hNCStrdjS0PSvGkY2gAnONg=&emci=332ca8cc-e9ef-ec11-b47a-281878b83d8a&emdi=c0cfd027-24f0-ec11-b47a-281878b83d8a&ceid=12666102)
Peter, today is Juneteenth, a day when we commemorate the emancipation of enslaved people in the United States.

Juneteenth is an occasion both for celebration and for remembrance. We will never know the loss and suffering that occurred in the two and a half years between the signing of the Emancipation Proclamation and this day in 1865, when the last slaves in Galveston, Texas were freed. This day serves as a reminder that mourning is inextricably linked with celebrating victories in the fight for racial justice in the United States.

So today, I am reminding myself of the responsibility we all have in the continued fight for racial equality. I will renew my willingness to fight, and I will refuse to let fear stop me. I invite you to join me in honoring the progress our nation has made since its founding and reflecting on the challenging and necessary work we have ahead of us as we strive for greater equality.

I hope you stand with me today and everyday moving forward.

Thank you for your time,

Yadira

| Yadira Caraveo is a pediatrician, state representative, and daughter of immigrants. Her parents raised her and her siblings on her father’s construction worker’s salary in Adams County. In the legislature, Dr. Caraveo has delivered for working Coloradans by standing up to the biggest corporate bullies and entrenched special interests.

Now, Dr. Caraveo is running for Congress to stand up for hard-working families she sees falling through the cracks. Dr. Caraveo would be the first Latina to represent Colorado in Congress and the only physician in Colorado’s congressional delegation. | |

Apologies for reaching out again, friend, but I need you to hear this important update.
_**FIRST:**_ I reached out to tell you that I was falling short of my midnight fundraising goal.
_**THEN:**_ Hundreds of grassroots supporters from all across the country stepped up to close my fundraising gap.
_**NOW:**_ I'm so close to reaching my critical goal (only 6% to go!), but I need just ONE more supporter from your area to help me get there before midnight.
**The GOP is trying to stack the cards against me, and I won't be able to counter their attacks if I don't hit tonight’s goal. **[Please, will you rush $5 or more to help me flip Florida blue and expand our Senate majority?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/46719039/351483127/-892300846?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk3577-fr-nd&recurring=0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84Mzc4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3ZjA2OWE4OS0yM2YwLWVjMTEtYjQ3YS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=LQhKwnPYZtlUy0WQ_yuXr3cH7YAlVsTVMJiAsxbs0-0=)

I'm worried, friend. With hours left to meet my midnight fundraising goal, **I'm still falling short.** Please, let me explain why this is such a crucial moment:
**In the race that will determine Senate control, I'm neck and neck with Marco Rubio – and top Democrats like Fred Guttenberg have said "we need an advocate like Val Demings, whose strength and integrity is exactly what Florida needs to replace him."**
The problem is that the GOP is stacking the cards against me because they KNOW this race is Democrats' best chance to expand our Senate majority. Far-right outside groups are dumping millions into Florida, and the Senate Republican campaign arm is funding a new ad buy to blanket the airwaves with personal attacks against me _._
**I'm looking at my fundraising numbers now, and I won't be able to defend myself from these GOP attacks if I don't reach my goal in the next few hours.**
**[Missing this goal and falling behind in this race could mean handing the Senate right back to Mitch McConnell, so I have to ask: Will you donate $5 or more to help me defeat Marco Rubio, flip Florida blue and expand our Senate majority?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/46719042/351483130/-892300846?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk3577-fr-nd&recurring=0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84Mzc4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3ZjA2OWE4OS0yM2YwLWVjMTEtYjQ3YS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=LQhKwnPYZtlUy0WQ_yuXr3cH7YAlVsTVMJiAsxbs0-0=)**
Thank you for your support,

Val Demings

Please, let me explain why this is such a crucial moment: In the race that will determine Senate control, I'm neck and neck with Marco Rubio – and top Democrats like Fred Guttenberg have said "we need an advocate like Val Demings, whose strength and integrity is exactly what Florida needs to replace him." The problem is that the GOP is stacking the cards against me because they KNOW this race is Democrats' best chance to expand our Senate majority. Far-right outside groups are dumping millions into Florida, and the Senate Republican campaign arm is funding a new ad buy to blanket the airwaves with personal attacks against me . I'm looking at my fundraising numbers now, and I won't be able to defend myself from these GOP attacks if I don't reach my goal in the next few hours. = Thank you for your support, Val Demings | | Our grassroots campaign is off to a great start, but in a race as close as this one, I need your help to surge ahead and defend the Senate. name: Sara Jacobs
subject: Happy Father’s Day!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Sara Jacobs for Congress
body:
I’m so grateful to have my dad as a role model for what service and showing up means. In honor of my dad, this Father’s Day, let’s remember how important it is to show up for each other in ways big and small.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/46719234/351484095/-1925218157?refcode=BLU-SJC-20220619-2-EM&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84OTMxMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhZmQ1NjA3Mi0yN2YwLWVjMTEtYjQ3YS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=UWeawFUN3k-be5SfPJAQKod7ZUkXRRv5KaAxlaV-z0Y=)
---
| Anyone who knows my dad, Gary, will say that he shows up for what he cares about. Whether that was staying up all night to help me write a paper, flying across the world to visit me when I lived abroad, or showing up to every single sports game – no matter how busy he was in his own life – my dad showed up for our family.

And that’s true for the community he loves as well.
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/46719235/351484096/-1925218157?refcode=BLU-SJC-20220619-2-EM&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84OTMxMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhZmQ1NjA3Mi0yN2YwLWVjMTEtYjQ3YS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=UWeawFUN3k-be5SfPJAQKod7ZUkXRRv5KaAxlaV-z0Y=)
He started a school to ensure kids are prepared for the careers of the future, and a program to bring Jewish and Arab teenagers together. He’s invested in developing products that will help all of us and has been a leader in San Diego and the Jewish community.

I’m so grateful to have my dad as a role model for what service and showing up means. In honor of my dad, this Father’s Day, let’s remember how important it is to show up for each other in ways big and small.

Happy Father’s Day to all the fathers and father figures celebrating. I hope you all have a day full of love and celebration!

With gratitude,

Sara

clean_body: I’m so grateful to have my dad as a role model for what service and showing up means. In honor of my dad, this Father’s Day, let’s remember how important it is to show up for each other in ways big and small. | = | Anyone who knows my dad, Gary, will say that he shows up for what he cares about. Whether that was staying up all night to help me write a paper, flying across the world to visit me when I lived abroad, or showing up to every single sports game – no matter how busy he was in his own life – my dad showed up for our family. And that’s true for the community he loves as well. = He started a school to ensure kids are prepared for the careers of the future, and a program to bring Jewish and Arab teenagers together. He’s invested in developing products that will help all of us and has been a leader in San Diego and the Jewish community. I’m so grateful to have my dad as a role model for what service and showing up means. In honor of my dad, this Father’s Day, let’s remember how important it is to show up for each other in ways big and small. Happy Father’s Day to all the fathers and father figures celebrating. I hope you all have a day full of love and celebration! With gratitude, Sara * | | = Paid for by Sara Jacobs for Congress Sara Jacobs for Congress 660 Pennsylvania Ave SE Suite 202 Washington, DC 20003 United States | | | ---|---|--- = If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please

name: Adam Kinzinger
subject: Juneteenth + 1/6 Digest
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Country First
body:
| [ ](http://www.country1st.com/?e=4ee51f15644d695f1e1564c20f506a7d&utm_source=country1st&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=fathersday_16_digest_nd&n=1)
---
Friend -- Happy Father's Day to all dads, including mine! I'm enjoying my first as a dad, so I'm going to make this note quick (although the great work of our team putting together the 1/6 Digest after each committee hearing is below). Today is also Juneteenth, a newer national holiday commemorating when enslaved people learned of their emancipation in 1865. America's legacy of slavery serves as a reminder that while the founding fathers of our nation may have devised the most inspirational and expansive set of rights and liberties the world had ever known, these didn't extend to everyone -- not even close. And so it is up to us to ensure the ideals and opportunity enshrined in our founding documents, and the amendments made over time, are available to ALL Americans -- no matter who they are or where they come from. While there's plenty of work still to do, we are a strong and good people, always in pursuit of a more perfect union, and there's nothing we can't do -- together. Country First, Adam
* * *
* * *
* * *
|
## 1/6 Committee Digest
Country First presents the next edition of The Digest to highlight the important work of the 1/6 Committee. Our aim is to make this available to our members within 24 hours after each hearing. **MARK YOUR CALENDARS FOR THE NEXT HEARING:** **TUESDAY, JUNE 21 AT 1 PM ET** **In Case You Missed It:**
* Rep. Adam Kinzinger appeared this morning on ABC's "This Week with George Stephanopoulos" to discuss the latest developments on the 1/6 Committee and threats to his family's life.
* Rep. Adam Kinzinger appeared on the June 12th episode of ["Face the Nation"](https://www.country1st.com/r?u=BiDEj8yGy-h4To0LUa2PcteaKinNaf6l7PNyCezzZS_1KaXSFyeg7D0Dd68Eywv1iHctMc3YqB4Ou3rLvAAWnlILcNkrk37kKU6RmnfXU76-xNs61di8kpS25mqfOIFj&e=4ee51f15644d695f1e1564c20f506a7d&utm_source=country1st&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=fathersday_16_digest_nd&n=2) to discuss the wider context of January 6th and Trump’s orchestrated lead-up to the attacks on the Capitol as well as the problem of loyalty to party or person over country and oath.
* Rep. Adam Kinzinger appeared on the June 14th episode of Fox News’s [“Your World”](https://www.country1st.com/r?u=KVYzWTFUuJ0SsyVZLAg-7Prqch2f6wpdYBztiC5xJrpfrRZPrT9Rwudvp1V2zP2DZZCUEEmHM--gBA7Np8029Iy5LaOi_w7cPdR2NAdT_CU&e=4ee51f15644d695f1e1564c20f506a7d&utm_source=country1st&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=fathersday_16_digest_nd&n=3) to discuss how the threat of January 6th isn’t over and is omnipresent.

**Who Gave Testimony in Hearing #2:**
* **Greg Jacob** , White House lawyer to former Vice President Pence
* **Judge Michael Luttig** , legal expert and advisor to Vice President Pence

Here are the major developments from the committee: **Judge Michael Luttig Stresses the Fact that Trump and his Allies Are Still a Threat** Wednesday’s hearing focused on how Trump and his allies attempted to coerce Mike Pence into illegally and unconstitutionally overturning the election results. On January 6th, Pence was in true danger and the mob came within 40 feet of him while he and his security detail moved to safety. At the end of Wednesday’s testimony, Michael Luttig, legal expert and former advisor to Mike Pence stated that Trump and his allies still pose [a clear and present danger](https://www.country1st.com/r?u=GjwzQmjoNJNKZ7-eUAZR_LydOlS6zrqei66ldwsr-YYF4MjZIFL23eZBWfZphZaWzDS2nbMfV3YO-lmIi0cz7g6CzrkWSvik-SOL71bK1AuPmoPm8ah9B1Ay8doIrs2jN8CCPxHLDDHDPeYiTZFIOiDQ6WbMd_wKj_J_ZRUO9_POOFrPPOcNQZUndr7zPuP4QNoN26n0fJmu12wsxuuq6Q&e=4ee51f15644d695f1e1564c20f506a7d&utm_source=country1st&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=fathersday_16_digest_nd&n=4) to the United States. Additionally, Luttig voiced his fears that if Trump were to run and lose again in the 2024 Presidential Election that he would most likely succeed in his renewed efforts to overturn the election results. **Legal Scholars agree that VP Pence did not have authority to decide the outcome of the 2020 election** Retired judge of the federal court of appeals, Judge J. Michael Luttig [debunked](https://www.country1st.com/r?u=oGkNRIpNU-65tzHrfmdwr9WX8R6QhZOqI-hEjFtQ9TOjXxGgHOdm1cKiMnSVeKuukapBgigQUOFdgdWJTNI6boxIlFLYYtVNOYdHM0UhFhY&e=4ee51f15644d695f1e1564c20f506a7d&utm_source=country1st&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=fathersday_16_digest_nd&n=5) the false legal theory that Trump lawyer John Eastman devised to grant the vice president the power to decide the next president of the United States. In challenging this legal theory, Luttig argues that the framers of the U.S. Constitution would not have granted one person the power to decide who the next president of the United States would be, citing the checks and balances system that the United States government is based upon. Greg Jacob, the former Counsel to Vice President Pence, [declared](https://www.country1st.com/r?u=oGkNRIpNU-65tzHrfmdwr5JDiszGo4s-jWOe6rvOAHWLTqr3e2rlQ-h5hq66_L-OJ5dvaL77-ORrhLrG87zThtFG4Phu4gbPC5SmEEoqh3uz0zA8dEOu0RV8TQ75yDh4op7F0fGnJZLykfKgFNhQ5zP6nzzclI1X2HxK1uEHEC_GePN6wwSUlcY16hYaUBpVQ5agEzXgtmlDJ2QaOr5yOpBKgifWEDL0zrBqyVv3Wa8&e=4ee51f15644d695f1e1564c20f506a7d&utm_source=country1st&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=fathersday_16_digest_nd&n=6) that "our review of text, history and frankly just common sense, all confirmed the vice president's first instinct on that point," which was that Pence did not have any authority to decide the outcome of the election. Ultimately, while some of the legal scholars who testified disagreed on the precise interpretation of the 12th amendment to the U.S. constitution, they all agreed that the amendment does not grant the vice president the authority to overturn election results or to decide the winner of the election. Retired conservative federal judge J. Michael Luttig gave testimony in which he said that if the vice president had gone along with Eastman’s scheme, it would have been "tantamount to a revolution," and "the first constitutional crisis since the founding of the republic."******Eastman Sought Presidential Pardon from Trump after Jan. 6 Attacks** In what could be interpreted as an acknowledgement of guilt and/or fear of prosecution, Trump lawyer John Eastman requested to"be on the pardon list" following the attack on the Capitol, via an [email to former Trump attorney](https://www.country1st.com/r?u=FECXgvVBAH18GBWRCy8D9bmQjRYvg5RiftfZZQBIKJ_Hq6_G1tVGbGkRwLQvIr58rQrfeynGNI5znXzJFP0u1NwYCSGgOufXXpt3HWvWYGXY9U7nhT2SYBFu1gimS5dh&e=4ee51f15644d695f1e1564c20f506a7d&utm_source=country1st&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=fathersday_16_digest_nd&n=7) Rudy Guiliani. Eastman was not granted a pardon, and it is unclear whether or not Guiliani took any action after receiving the email. Congressman Jamie Raskin (D-MD) stated "the fact that he was looking into a pardon for himself as a lawyer suggests either consciousness of guilt or fear that he might be guilty." The lawyer also [knew](https://www.country1st.com/r?u=9j2xrPCwd3yFLU_EZoBgHvHQuaY08Ikkppz5B9JsddJxKrmM5rgZ1VFafZHAAcy7l-f5XuWwwg7T3YeDDtz2TOZbt4wdDMyu62QiD0hrkAU4_Y0djxTiMj7_YLFS77QT&e=4ee51f15644d695f1e1564c20f506a7d&utm_source=country1st&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=fathersday_16_digest_nd&n=8) that the former President’s actions were illegal, according to former Vice President Pence’s top legal advisor. In a White House discussion just days before the violent attack, Eastman admitted to the illegality of the former President’s scheme to retain power, however the former President dismissed the warning. **Former Vice President was just Steps Away From the Angry Mob that Wanted to "Hang Him"** After harrowing new video and photos were released by the 1/6 Committee, the American public finally knows just how close the former Vice President was to an angry mob that wanted to hang him for refusing to decertify the election. A [mere 40 feet](https://www.country1st.com/r?u=9j2xrPCwd3yFLU_EZoBgHvHQuaY08Ikkppz5B9JsddJxKrmM5rgZ1VFafZHAAcy7l-f5XuWwwg7T3YeDDtz2TOZbt4wdDMyu62QiD0hrkAU4_Y0djxTiMj7_YLFS77QT&e=4ee51f15644d695f1e1564c20f506a7d&utm_source=country1st&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=fathersday_16_digest_nd&n=9) stood between the second-in-command of the nation and a group of far-right conspiracy theorists that were hunting him for upholding the Constitution. After being told the insurrectionists had breached the Capitol, the then-Vice President and his family were evacuated to a secure location; a [parking garage](https://www.country1st.com/r?u=GjwzQmjoNJNKZ7-eUAZR_PA_nNH0Da_jCJ3Safw4xOswqalaVnbToNKQIMK44wGhKn7Y9CTdc1WCx3DIOQ2gQ30j1zyep6KRb5OQVQArregH2H81tVd_80R54NSlxXJJ-MSqhZE6gg6Zy3L67A5RfNxoJk89rD7AxhmtHRUjWPc&e=4ee51f15644d695f1e1564c20f506a7d&utm_source=country1st&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=fathersday_16_digest_nd&n=10) and loading dock. Despite pleas from his Secret Service, Pence [refused](https://www.country1st.com/r?u=Y5Xlf9pA_AJcYmYaz2zF4Kh8oXkJkDGpym418fA-N3kc3fE1Q5rodUdYndjCjvip2pYnfwFOIdQBIlRqdRJhJzhLGjTqH7SVr-_R6P2WpKU&e=4ee51f15644d695f1e1564c20f506a7d&utm_source=country1st&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=fathersday_16_digest_nd&n=11) to leave the Capitol complex, telling his agent, "I know you, I trust you, but you're not the one behind the wheel." **1/6 Committee Has Requested Ginni Thomas Testimony ** **Committee chair Bennie Thompson (D-Miss), along with Vice Chair Liz Cheney (R-Wyo)[ extended an invitation to Ginni Thomas](https://www.country1st.com/r?u=ByhkdA7RrYhHtdiUMWUYvDoU31aLiS3EgmUxpTQDTvEUu2MAKZsZkRx03bTEmdiWbEFA1jhiySV0cHPuxtLrnzePbEdvsKavTzC2SfTgBva-PFBX6lRZlfww_K3XjYI22M4mgvMr-5N0A2Lz_uu3nw&e=4ee51f15644d695f1e1564c20f506a7d&utm_source=country1st&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=fathersday_16_digest_nd&n=12) early in the hearing Thursday to testify regarding her connection to the Jan. 6th attacks. ** **Recent reports have revealed potential communication and email between Thomas and Trump lawyer John Eastman, who concocted an illegal and unconstitutional plan for Trump to cling to power.** **Ginni Thomas is the wife of Supreme Court Justice Clarence Thomas. Eastman is former Supreme Court Clerk to Justice Thomas.**
* * *
**MARK YOUR CALENDARS FOR THE NEXT HEARING:** **TUESDAY, JUNE 21 AT 1 PM ET** _Country First is proud to release The Weekly Digest with important updates on the 1/6 Committee's investigation of the Capitol attack. If you would like to help contribute content to this newsletter, please e-mail[volunteer@country1st.com](mailto:volunteer@country1st.com)._

clean_body: | Friend -- Happy Father's Day to all dads, including mine! I'm enjoying my first as a dad, so I'm going to make this note quick (although the great work of our team putting together the 1/6 Digest after each committee hearing is below). Today is also Juneteenth, a newer national holiday commemorating when enslaved people learned of their emancipation in 1865. America's legacy of slavery serves as a reminder that while the founding fathers of our nation may have devised the most inspirational and expansive set of rights and liberties the world had ever known, these didn't extend to everyone -- not even close. And so it is up to us to ensure the ideals and opportunity enshrined in our founding documents, and the amendments made over time, are available to ALL Americans -- no matter who they are or where they come from. While there's plenty of work still to do, we are a strong and good people, always in pursuit of a more perfect union, and there's nothing we can't do -- together. Country First, Adam | 1/6 Committee Digest Country First presents the next edition of The Digest to highlight the important work of the 1/6 Committee. Our aim is to make this available to our members within 24 hours after each hearing. MARK YOUR CALENDARS FOR THE NEXT HEARING: TUESDAY, JUNE 21 AT 1 PM ET In Case You Missed It: Rep. Adam Kinzinger appeared this morning on ABC's "This Week with George Stephanopoulos" to discuss the latest developments on the 1/6 Committee and threats to his family's life. Rep. Adam Kinzinger appeared on the June 12th episode of to discuss the wider context of January 6th and Trump’s orchestrated lead-up to the attacks on the Capitol as well as the problem of loyalty to party or person over country and oath. Rep. Adam Kinzinger appeared on the June 14th episode of Fox News’s to discuss how the threat of January 6th isn’t over and is omnipresent. Who Gave Testimony in Hearing #2: Greg Jacob , White House lawyer to former Vice President Pence Judge Michael Luttig , legal expert and advisor to Vice President Pence Here are the major developments from the committee: Judge Michael Luttig Stresses the Fact that Trump and his Allies Are Still a Threat Wednesday’s hearing focused on how Trump and his allies attempted to coerce Mike Pence into illegally and unconstitutionally overturning the election results. On January 6th, Pence was in true danger and the mob came within 40 feet of him while he and his security detail moved to safety. At the end of Wednesday’s testimony, Michael Luttig, legal expert and former advisor to Mike Pence stated that Trump and his allies still pose to the United States. Additionally, Luttig voiced his fears that if Trump were to run and lose again in the 2024 Presidential Election that he would most likely succeed in his renewed efforts to overturn the election results. Legal Scholars agree that VP Pence did not have authority to decide the outcome of the 2020 election Retired judge of the federal court of appeals, Judge J. Michael Luttig the false legal theory that Trump lawyer John Eastman devised to grant the vice president the power to decide the next president of the United States. In challenging this legal theory, Luttig argues that the framers of the U.S. Constitution would not have granted one person the power to decide who the next president of the United States would be, citing the checks and balances system that the United States government is based upon. Greg Jacob, the former Counsel to Vice President Pence, that "our review of text, history and frankly just common sense, all confirmed the vice president's first instinct on that point," which was that Pence did not have any authority to decide the outcome of the election. Ultimately, while some of the legal scholars who testified disagreed on the precise interpretation of the 12th amendment to the U.S. constitution, they all agreed that the amendment does not grant the vice president the authority to overturn election results or to decide the winner of the election. Retired conservative federal judge J. Michael Luttig gave testimony in which he said that if the vice president had gone along with Eastman’s scheme, it would have been "tantamount to a revolution," and "the first constitutional crisis since the founding of the republic."****Eastman Sought Presidential Pardon from Trump after Jan. 6 Attacks In what could be interpreted as an acknowledgement of guilt and/or fear of prosecution, Trump lawyer John Eastman requested to"be on the pardon list" following the attack on the Capitol, via an Rudy Guiliani. Eastman was not granted a pardon, and it is unclear whether or not Guiliani took any action after receiving the email. Congressman Jamie Raskin (D-MD) stated "the fact that he was looking into a pardon for himself as a lawyer suggests either consciousness of guilt or fear that he might be guilty." The lawyer also that the former President’s actions were illegal, according to former Vice President Pence’s top legal advisor. In a White House discussion just days before the violent attack, Eastman admitted to the illegality of the former President’s scheme to retain power, however the former President dismissed the warning. Former Vice President was just Steps Away From the Angry Mob that Wanted to "Hang Him" After harrowing new video and photos were released by the 1/6 Committee, the American public finally knows just how close the former Vice President was to an angry mob that wanted to hang him for refusing to decertify the election. A stood between the second-in-command of the nation and a group of far-right conspiracy theorists that were hunting him for upholding the Constitution. After being told the insurrectionists had breached the Capitol, the then-Vice President and his family were evacuated to a secure location; a and loading dock. Despite pleas from his Secret Service, Pence to leave the Capitol complex, telling his agent, "I know you, I trust you, but you're not the one behind the wheel." 1/6 Committee Has Requested Ginni Thomas Testimony Committee chair Bennie Thompson (D-Miss), along with Vice Chair Liz Cheney (R-Wyo) early in the hearing Thursday to testify regarding her connection to the Jan. 6th attacks. Recent reports have revealed potential communication and email between Thomas and Trump lawyer John Eastman, who concocted an illegal and unconstitutional plan for Trump to cling to power. Ginni Thomas is the wife of Supreme Court Justice Clarence Thomas. Eastman is former Supreme Court Clerk to Justice Thomas. MARK YOUR CALENDARS FOR THE NEXT HEARING: TUESDAY, JUNE 21 AT 1 PM ET Country First is proud to release The Weekly Digest with important updates on the 1/6 Committee's investigation of the Capitol attack. If you would like to help contribute content to this newsletter, please e-mailmailto:volunteer@country1st.com. | "Get Involved" "Support the Mission" | "Wear the Cause" Paid for by Country First Country First · PO Box 2385, Ottawa, IL 61350, United States

name: =?utf-8?Q?Kevin=20Mullin?=
subject: Happy Fathers’ Day
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY KEVIN MULLIN FOR CONGRESS
body:
| | | | [ ](https://kevinmullinforcongress.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=31b479dbea5973b27a9d19670&id=7f89f15f30&e=292fde286a)
---
| | | Dear Peter,

Today is Fathers’ Day.

I am so grateful to be the father to twin sons, Liam and Landon.

Like all fathers and father figures, we do our level best to give our children the best lives we can, and that takes sacrifice and work every day to build a better future and world for the next generation to inherit.

That means delivering bold solutions on climate change, ensuring child care and health care are accessible and affordable, and making sure the America we pass on is one where every woman has the freedom to control their bodies and reproductive choices. It’s on us, dads, to make sure our democracy is strong, and every one of our children has the opportunity to get ahead. Simply put, we owe this much to the next generation.

That’s why I’m running for Congress. My sons Liam and Landon, their generation, and future generations are counting on us — depending on us — to deliver.

Wishing you and your family a Happy Fathers’ Day,

Kevin Mullin
---
