name: Joy Thomas Moore
subject: Reflecting on Father’s Day
body:
| [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/46717551/351475007/1627778034?refcode=fl_em_na_20220619fathersday_jtm_120da_xx_hdr&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDQ1NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkMmUxMzQzYS0wY2YwLWVjMTEtYjQ3YS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=yAzUohV3yhgYhUQ7r3Q6Vo1UmCk5gMEoK2OyIGNUMb8=)
---
Hello friend, Today is Father’s Day, a time for celebrating all the fathers, grandfathers, and father figures out there who love and support their families in so many ways. On this day, I’m reminded of my husband and Wes’ father, Westley. He was a hardworking reporter and devoted dad with a gentle, calming presence. Naturally, he was a role model and hero to our son. But, sadly, he would not be in Wes’ life for long. | [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/46717552/351475008/1627778034?refcode=fl_em_na_20220619fathersday_jtm_120da_xx_img1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDQ1NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkMmUxMzQzYS0wY2YwLWVjMTEtYjQ3YS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=yAzUohV3yhgYhUQ7r3Q6Vo1UmCk5gMEoK2OyIGNUMb8=)
---
Wes was only three years old when he lost his dad. None of us will forget that night. After feeling sick for a day with a sore throat and fever, Westley decided to drive himself to the hospital. His symptoms were not taken seriously and he was told to go home and “get some sleep.” Hours later, Westley collapsed down the stairs, gasping for air. By the time the ambulance made it to the hospital, he was already gone.
Losing a loved one is never easy, but it’s especially shocking when it’s so sudden. And, it’s infuriating when it could have been prevented. Westley passed from a rare but treatable virus, leaving Wes to live the rest of his life without his father by his side.
Raising Wes and his two sisters wasn’t easy as a widowed mother. I can’t count the times I wished his father was there to provide him patience and guidance. Though Wes was a handful in his adolescent years, he ultimately turned his path around in military school. He went on to become a captain in the U.S. Army, a Rhodes Scholar, a White House Fellow, a small business owner, and an executive at one of the nation’s leading non-profit organizations. Most importantly, he became a husband and a father.
I’ve had the honor of watching my son become an extraordinary dad to his own son and daughter. It’s a joy seeing Wes pass down the lessons that Westley and I tried to instill in him. I wish his dad was here to see him and his grandchildren now, but I know he’s watching and cheering on in spirit.
[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/46717553/351475009/1627778034?refcode=fl_em_na_20220619fathersday_jtm_120da_xx_img2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDQ1NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkMmUxMzQzYS0wY2YwLWVjMTEtYjQ3YS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=yAzUohV3yhgYhUQ7r3Q6Vo1UmCk5gMEoK2OyIGNUMb8=)
---
It is not only Father’s Day, but Juneteenth. Long recognized in the African-American community, it is now also, and rightfully, a federal holiday. Juneteenth celebrates the hope and promise of freedom. Yet, while we honor progress of the past, we keep in mind the continued challenges facing us. There is ongoing work that must be done to achieve justice and equity. Though slavery was abolished, racial disparities – in housing, education, environment, economic opportunity, and health care – still remain.
Yet today is largely one of gratitude. We remember and show appreciation. I’m proud to watch Wes lovingly raise his children. And, at the same time, work so hard to create a brighter future for them, and every Marylander.
Happy Juneteenth, and enjoy Father’s Day!
Thanks for reading,
Joy
[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/46717554/351475010/1627778034?refcode=fl_em_na_20220619fathersday_jtm_120da_xx_img3&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDQ1NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkMmUxMzQzYS0wY2YwLWVjMTEtYjQ3YS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=yAzUohV3yhgYhUQ7r3Q6Vo1UmCk5gMEoK2OyIGNUMb8=)
---
| Wes Moore is a combat veteran, a former small business owner, and the former CEO of a leading non-profit. Wes believes no matter where you start in life, you deserve an equal opportunity to succeed. He’s running to become Governor of Maryland and flip this state blue for the first time in 8 years. And we need your help to do it.

body:
The people have spoken: it’s time to RETIRE Nancy Pelosi.| | | Peter, The people have spoken: it’s time to RETIRE Nancy Pelosi.

---
[](http://links.hungforcongress.com/u/click?_t=c514f30bd6cd4b1bb0e09a0456675c53&_m=458e4fa162ec4b99a08368070867a12a&_e=2m72yTmk47322MfmmN1bvP0e8oahOLrqBKjOULvXfB36bXR29YncJMWvrnDCaIZkI4ilVwDIDzL8_6RzlIOXBDhvr4x11iUnfgPzGLnJPEr-sI4ByIQyeESKpm74NcCJECkhdnljD60_kzoCPi0uaazAH_3zrKJ6yUEd24Oyww6t6QjttocFn9ZdC64xZls2u54TPiU3bvbhxfPDFh4b3vxvVaIY2HRuWE_iYSLddCmWVx2I5-Pbs5GG31ajIV7b659xRDbCmi8ENM2J1I7_QNkM9miX0JTeGe-3UZtMMaCZrGNEjBsVnYMI0d5SkNeZqw-_KeeZtdakZtASBWwY5Ta-zQ8G4-U5f2ufTAfUX5m4kPhnNyjnRarSQAw0PD3REu1sHJJKbnEoneAJS9LTqXcv4mNGM8fOJJHFCRu5PNI%3D)

For too long, we’ve been at the whims of her far Left agenda.

name: Team Abrams
subject: will you be one of the 7 donors from your area today?
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ABRAMS FOR GOVERNOR |
body:
[1]Stacey Abrams for Governor

Derek,

FIRST: The Associated Press said that our race was likely to be [ https://act.staceyabrams.com/go/1438?refcode=sa_em_220619_B2_Zip_S1_T1_TA_30open_RS&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&addr1=&city=&state=&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amount=5&amounts=5%2C25%2C50%2C100%2C250&t=1002&akid=682%2E1862389%2EzxfXkf ]one of
the most expensive in the nation — because the other side has millions of
dollars. Election analysts are warning the race could come down to just a
few thousand votes.

THEN: Brian Kemp’s team publicly vowed to spend “whatever it takes” to
beat us.

ALREADY: He’s spent millions of dollars on an expansive organizing program
— and flooded the airwaves with negative ads.

But Derek,
we’ve done the math, and if just 7 supporters from
your area
stepped up and donated today, we’d be able to blow past our goals for the
month and close the fundraising gap.

[ https://act.staceyabrams.com/go/1438?refcode=sa_em_220619_B2_Zip_S1_T1_TA_30open_RS&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&addr1=&city=&state=&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amount=5&amounts=5%2C25%2C50%2C100%2C250&t=1003&akid=682%2E1862389%2EzxfXkf ]Derek, will
you be one of the 7 folks from
your area
that we need? As a grassroots campaign, we’re counting on
supporters like you
today.

Derek, you can
see the effects of Kemp’s money machine in real time. Already, recent
polling has shown him with a slight edge.

But that’s why Stacey is committed to traveling to every corner of the
state and to lift up the voices of Georgians who Kemp has left behind. We
have a plan to reach voters that most campaigns ignore and write off — but
as you can imagine, it isn’t cheap or easy.

And with our first public fundraising deadline of the general election in
just a few weeks, we need your support more than ever,
Derek. At the end of the month,
all of our numbers will go public for everyone (including Brian Kemp and
his Republican allies) to see.

[ https://act.staceyabrams.com/go/1438?refcode=sa_em_220619_B2_Zip_S1_T1_TA_30open_RS&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&addr1=&city=&state=&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amount=5&amounts=5%2C25%2C50%2C100%2C250&t=1004&akid=682%2E1862389%2EzxfXkf ]This campaign relies on grassroots donors to power our work. When Brian
Kemp is looking at our fundraising numbers at the end of the month, will
you be one of the donors from
your area
that he sees?

Thank you for all that you do,

Team Abrams

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

+---------------------------------+
| PAID FOR BY ABRAMS FOR GOVERNOR |
+---------------------------------+

Abrams for Governor
931 Monroe Dr NE
Ste A102 #363
Atlanta, GA 30308
United States

Contributions to Abrams for Governor are not tax deductible.

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is one of the most critical
tools we have to reach supporters like you, but you can click here to let
us know if you would like to [ https://act.staceyabrams.com/go/1468?t=1001&akid=682%2E1862389%2EzxfXkf ]receive fewer emails or

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://act.staceyabrams.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=682%2E1862389%2EzxfXkf

name: Our Revolution
subject: Howard Schultz is a literal monster
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Our Revolution click here to unsubscribe
body:
[ stdin# ][IMG]

https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/5943?t=1008&akid=3054%2E5885482%2E67w57D

Peter Willis,

Howard Schultz, a ludicrously out-of-touch billionaire CEO (who Hillary Clinton allegedly planned on installing as the Secretary of Labor if she didn’t run the single worst campaign in modern history and lose to Donald Trump) is getting his butt kicked by Starbucks workers in their fight to unionize.

That’s why Bernie Sanders is touring America to stand up to corporate power and demand CEOs – like Howard Schultz – stop UNION-BUSTING.

Howard Schultz lied to union organizers and ok’d retaliation tactics like firing workers and threatening to strip healthcare for transgender employees. We can’t let this corporate thuggery stand.

Our Revolution is standing with Bernie to stop the corporate assault on workers’ rights. We’re leading the fight to help low-wage workers ORGANIZE by advocating for real labor law reform on the state and federal levels! (https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/5943?t=1009&akid=3054%2E5885482%2E67w57D)

If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation to Our Revolution will process immediately.
CHIP IN $5 NOW » (https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/5944?refcode=OR_EM_FR_45SP_202206019_2_6&t=1010&akid=3054%2E5885482%2E67w57D)
CHIP IN $18 NOW » (https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/5945?refcode=OR_EM_FR_45SP_202206019_2_6&t=1011&akid=3054%2E5885482%2E67w57D)
CHIP IN $27 NOW » (https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/5946?refcode=OR_EM_FR_45SP_202206019_2_6&t=1012&akid=3054%2E5885482%2E67w57D)
CHIP IN $100 NOW » (https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/5947?refcode=OR_EM_FR_45SP_202206019_2_6&t=1013&akid=3054%2E5885482%2E67w57D)
CHIP IN $250 NOW » (https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/5948?refcode=OR_EM_FR_45SP_202206019_2_6&t=1014&akid=3054%2E5885482%2E67w57D)
CHIP IN ANOTHER AMOUNT » (https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/5949?refcode=OR_EM_FR_45SP_202206019_2_6&t=1015&akid=3054%2E5885482%2E67w57D)

The sad truth is that for the past few decades, wages have not kept up with the rising costs of healthcare, education, childcare, and other basic necessities for working families.

The reason is simple: Union membership in the US has been pushed to record lows.

The resurgence of the labor movement happening now in this country is a beacon of strength for our movement.

Stand with Our Revolution as we support Starbucks workers - and all workers who are being crushed by corporate greed. Rush a donation to help us take on corporate power and WIN!

In solidarity,

Our Revolution

Do you? | Ambassador Nikki Haley Senator Steve Daines Doug Flutie Senator Tim Scott Newt Gingrich Senator Lindsey Graham Senator Joni Ernst What do all these conservative leaders have in common? They've endorsed my campaign! Please take a look at a short video they recorded for you, Peter: I am honored to have so many strong conservatives by my side, but here's the truth of the matter: this race is too close to call. Millions and millions of dollars are flooding Georgia to keep freshman Democrat Raphael Warnock in the Senate. Our opponent already has $23 million in the bank, and if he keeps up his fundraising pace, he'll have millions more by the end of the month. So many folks are asking you to get involved in this race - not to sit on the sidelines. Since they've asked, I want to ask you one more time too: Thanks, Herschel | | PAID FOR BY TEAM HERSCHEL, INC. name: Nan Whaley
subject: What happens next if the Supreme Court strikes down Roe
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Whaley/Stephens
body:
So we have to elect people we can count on.

Team,
After the recent leak of a draft Supreme Court decision devastating _Roe v. Wade_ , we know what may be next: The right to access the full range of reproductive care, including abortion, will vary state to state depending on who’s in power.
We can’t count on Mike DeWine or the Republican legislature in Ohio to protect our rights. In fact, they’re already hearing testimony on a bill that would ban abortion outright in Ohio.
**[It is more critical than ever that we elect a governor who will protect reproductive freedom. I am running to be that governor – pitch in to my campaign right now.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/46719898/351508789/-293918599?refcode=nw_email_220619_1_t2&amount=5&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&city=&state=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODM5MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4NmMxMTliNC0zNWYwLWVjMTEtYjQ3YS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=obklZ4Y-LsTjW1QnZMcH7zVeH59-vlTwVwZpUtB876g=)**
The plain truth is that politicians don’t belong in our medical decisions. But by criminalizing abortion, that’s exactly what Mike DeWine and his allies are doing – inserting themselves into our medical care.
We can’t count on the Supreme Court to protect our rights. We can’t count on Republicans in the state legislature, and we definitely can’t count on Mike DeWine. So we have to elect people we _can_ count on.
**[Ohio is a pro-choice state, and we must keep it that way. Rush $5 to my campaign>> ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/46719899/351508790/-293918599?refcode=nw_email_220619_1_t2&amount=5&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&city=&state=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODM5MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4NmMxMTliNC0zNWYwLWVjMTEtYjQ3YS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=obklZ4Y-LsTjW1QnZMcH7zVeH59-vlTwVwZpUtB876g=)**
Onward,

Nan Whaley

Hi Friend,

I’m not sure if you’ve seen this yet, but U.S. Senator Ted Cruz endorsed my campaign to help us take back Virginia’s 7th Congressional District and unseat Abigail Spanberger.

Here’s what the Senator had to say in case you missed it!
[](http://links.yeslivegaforvirginia.com/u/click?_t=876a4969a9724ab8bc814d84bfee8268&_m=e4b86cd0d73548319c2671a001d9b28b&_e=2LoReYBUBuITNZRkvvTXoUPOPMPL_Y1WvGsTufJqHc31PfXLnjRUGmRB8y2_EzSIfGtR4bq26n61KdUoNzO6xlssgdy6X3uBDICbQ2CvPyLfW2GOWQ1EBFt29sJbwYSvgMZ_NXVZ4C7v8ueGsruAVpvStmsvlDJ6FA-tMX4fRDipe0wQhEOLDBQEuIXqI1EiXBqh5D3y6QrC46WpGaiK6UuiVBzf1icKwp0VAjoSexUSK9FM8ike-ryR7hjyoBXrND-XXZ-j2NU-iQT4PIuiysXHRnM0St7VDbAsWfe-heAQcG_t9qI0jUlL4uKAxvC9pltkT9nOSPV6hNTQC6EVScpnB0DrwvoU0aNigG0Zqmro2AHepStmfE1j5Iz0IWP1CRxDoW3O8coLQGJb6o1Op3Z6ElUh5b1xVSqd0H3fMP4mqH9g2sWS1W-evDF3atL-CUPDo_n_9MQMw7cwZfTBMHOMpObJBtplp8QgP-2iFS1p3_E5RTalTXiJdc8ZrCJkw1IPIkLOq9g_vSWa9OmxI5KiPGt7M6cRA8H9ysAFMdg%3D)
**Last year, Senator Cruz bet early on Glenn Youngkin and won. Now it’s our turn.**

**[I hope we can earn your vote on June 21st!](http://links.yeslivegaforvirginia.com/u/click?_t=876a4969a9724ab8bc814d84bfee8268&_m=e4b86cd0d73548319c2671a001d9b28b&_e=2LoReYBUBuITNZRkvvTXoUPOPMPL_Y1WvGsTufJqHc31PfXLnjRUGmRB8y2_EzSIfGtR4bq26n61KdUoNzO6xlssgdy6X3uBDICbQ2CvPyLfW2GOWQ1EBFt29sJbwYSvgMZ_NXVZ4C7v8ueGsruAVpvStmsvlDJ6FA-tMX4fRDipe0wQhEOLDBQEuIXqI1EiXBqh5D3y6QrC46WpGaiK6UuiVBzf1icKwp0VAjoSexUSK9FM8ike-ryR7hjyoBXrND-XXZ-j2NU-iQT4PIuiysXHRnM0St7VDbAsWfe-heAQcG_t9qI0jUlL4uKAxvC9BW4cyMxDejKLgj5-Iomy-MxA3BrtfJTam-6bHlr7xojPepuBok9Bg4EDm8nN3M0xkxEDM_J78UwI8i4hr3pZLopf9wZ8gHxp4Sg6NQ3nDkli_8ojh6lZw8K7n02h9PDGyCVkpa-ni3xHo5N7VF2eRphPts1ASxGvnv74jpc30lCxijlH95mnZx0aFmQOaelXdO1UM9lRhuBZOgdqsxiTZkfsO9UIdKKIYPcKLoHIUHQ%3D)**

Thanks for your time,

Yesli Vega

name: Ken Russell
subject: Celebrating Juneteenth
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Ken Russell for Florida
body:
On June 19th, we celebrate an important milestone in American history. The day that the last enslaved people gained freedom in the United States. On J | | | [](http://links.kenrussellforfloridaemail.com/u/click?_t=cee06b13e3c1411baa566b417c320e45&_m=9987667c00ed49208492f29a82c686fd&_e=YtJvpO6MgfjzwCRz7RMNxaDLzGn-nfi8xlKwNlngWU-9UzNA7ANqviLuonNI0kcyBrgnCZ27m1keD29McSV2Fq9XJ5TcGOAkQmp2c510PW5F9DlXJKgJKsU--kBNciKVzJGSxO-7wAtiqLYl5OLtTgl3YU0zgWeik-UWR_Nqtlwdre7__3lMS5BPYikkKxPkWTek-r7t45Pox-KOElRf3x49Pqdk38nYSIGegayWRJG_6u-YL_VnLf44heRI26dMpr4NURPk7A68dSGwfUZRig%3D%3D) Dear Peter, On June 19th, we celebrate an important milestone in American history. The day that the last enslaved people gained freedom in the United States. On June 19th, 1866, several years AFTER enslaved people in the United States were emancipated, the last of those who had been held hostage by slavers heard about and gained their freedom in our country. **On that day, we took a significant step forward along the path to justice**. Today, we remember a moment when America moved forward, beginning the long journey of reconciling with our country's atrocities. We have continued to take steps toward justice, but we still have **a long way to go** to right the wrongs of our history and build a truly empowered multi-racial democracy. Our nation could be a progressive social democracy built on a solid foundation of justice and compassion. Today, on Juneteenth, we dare to dream of a brighter future in which every American is granted the same liberties, opportunities, and freedoms. Regardless of race, background, gender, religion, disability, sexual orientation, or anything else. Happy Juneteenth. Solidarity forever, Ken Russell Paid for by Ken Russell for Florida
---
This email was sent to [dpwillis67@gmail.com](mailto:dpwillis67@gmail.com). Don't want to receive these emails anymore? [Unsubscribe](http://links.kenrussellforfloridaemail.com/e/encryptedUnsubscribe?_r=cee06b13e3c1411baa566b417c320e45&_s=9987667c00ed49208492f29a82c686fd&_t=u2vxlp08jpKJn2WDQd4OeqxAfkBPicTuvPYXs4REOxwZ7zChskOtk6t0BxbkWh8_nGHK4947ttGCtbQThnkFUjzcErFv39MSfAwAb5LyQEW4YnyZqmq6RPyqWBj0EunqcWy1M3pLs8JmQVGW2yIQ0bGh_SlcZv-5RIqNm5Z8gMo%3D)

clean_body: All these folks are supporting our campaign for United States Senate. Do you? | Ambassador Nikki Haley Senator Steve Daines Doug Flutie Senator Tim Scott Newt Gingrich Senator Lindsey Graham Senator Joni Ernst What do all these conservative leaders have in common? They've endorsed my campaign! Please take a look at a short video they recorded for you, Peter: I am honored to have so many strong conservatives by my side, but here's the truth of the matter: this race is too close to call. Millions and millions of dollars are flooding Georgia to keep freshman Democrat Raphael Warnock in the Senate. Our opponent already has $23 million in the bank, and if he keeps up his fundraising pace, he'll have millions more by the end of the month. So many folks are asking you to get involved in this race - not to sit on the sidelines. Since they've asked, I want to ask you one more time too: Thanks, Herschel | | PAID FOR BY TEAM HERSCHEL, INC. Don't want to receive any more of our emails? We hate to see you go, but ._

subject: Dishonesty in Prime Time

body:
| | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/heidistjohnforcongress/dishonesty-in-prime-time?e=b0bcc6b4f9)
---
| | |
---
| | | Hi Friends,

Political Theater

Both the Democrats and RINOs have become political thespians. Perhaps there is no greater example than the January 6th “hearings” taking place in Washington.

Never was there a more dishonest sham of an “investigation” in our nation’s history. The committee has no interest in the Truth and no stomach for an honest examination of fact. The committee was carefully chosen and filled only with those who hate Donald Trump and hate the Republican Party.

This prime time display of dishonest, partisan politics is an embarrassment to any who love liberty and all who love Truth. It’s a show as carefully orchestrated as any Broadway musical to mesmerize gullible Americans, to avoid the uncomfortable Truth, and to stir up hatred and division.

Sadly, this is only the latest in a long line of political theater. Truth has become the victim in this relentless drama as those who proclaim themselves as “America’s leaders” obfuscate facts, manipulate details, and bury the Truth under layer after layer of lies and half-truths.

My campaign for Congress is a journey toward restoration of honesty and truthfulness in politics. I’m fighting against candidates who have repeatedly misrepresented themselves and donned costumes of conservatism in their attempt to deceive the voters of Washington’s 3rd Congressional District.

As the curtain falls on this latest Washington production of the January 6th follies, just remember that electing political actors is what’s gotten America into this mess and electing different actors is only going to make our perilous situation more dire.

Washington State is worth fighting for—and together, we can restore law and order to our nation and our state. Please help me get to Congress by [donating here](https://heidistjohnforcongress.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=46e715d1574a19c15defda548&id=75cb0d97cb&e=b0bcc6b4f9). Sincerely,
---
|

_Candidate for Congress, WA-03_

Summer is the battleground season for campaigns. It’s when we solidify our primaries, fire up our voters, and reach those undecided citizens who are the key to winning tight races.

Democrats are _losing it_ , Peter.

They’re ignoring when families struggle with inflation.

They’re infiltrating your children's education.

name: Karen Bass
subject: Happy Juneteenth to all!
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY KAREN BASS FOR MAYOR 2022 – GENERAL
body:
Join me in celebrating and learning this Juneteenth.

Team,

I believe one of the most important things we can do is accurately and honestly understand our country's past.

Last year, I proudly stood in the United States Capitol — a building built by Black Americans who were enslaved — and voted to make Juneteenth a national holiday. I watched President Biden sign that bill into law. And tomorrow, our nation will officially celebrate Juneteenth as a federal holiday.

Juneteenth was initially celebrated in Galveston, Texas, after Major General Gordon Granger led Union troops into the city to announce the end of the Civil War and President Lincoln's emancipation proclamation formally ending slavery a full two years after both had occurred.

It is a day to celebrate liberation and to remember the ultimate sacrifices made by Union soldiers, notably more than 200,000 Black soldiers, to deliver freedom to those who were formerly enslaved.

**No one can erase the truths of our nation’s history.** And we cannot distance our past from the present. The legacy of chattel slavery lives on and has shaped, in part, the world we live in today. Recognizing Juneteenth — National Freedom Day — as a national holiday is an important step in addressing injustice.

Today, as we reflect on our past, we must be honest and forthright about troubling parts of our nation's story and recognize the strength and courage of Black Americans. Today, we acknowledge National Freedom Day with an unwavering commitment to a more just society.

Wishing you a joyful celebration of Juneteenth,

Karen

P.S. Juneteenth falling on Father’s Day adds an extra level of reflection to such a special day. clean_body: Join me in celebrating and learning this Juneteenth. Team, I believe one of the most important things we can do is accurately and honestly understand our country's past. Last year, I proudly stood in the United States Capitol — a building built by Black Americans who were enslaved — and voted to make Juneteenth a national holiday. I watched President Biden sign that bill into law. And tomorrow, our nation will officially celebrate Juneteenth as a federal holiday. Juneteenth was initially celebrated in Galveston, Texas, after Major General Gordon Granger led Union troops into the city to announce the end of the Civil War and President Lincoln's emancipation proclamation formally ending slavery a full two years after both had occurred. It is a day to celebrate liberation and to remember the ultimate sacrifices made by Union soldiers, notably more than 200,000 Black soldiers, to deliver freedom to those who were formerly enslaved. No one can erase the truths of our nation’s history. And we cannot distance our past from the present. The legacy of chattel slavery lives on and has shaped, in part, the world we live in today. Recognizing Juneteenth — National Freedom Day — as a national holiday is an important step in addressing injustice. Today, as we reflect on our past, we must be honest and forthright about troubling parts of our nation's story and recognize the strength and courage of Black Americans. Today, we acknowledge National Freedom Day with an unwavering commitment to a more just society. Wishing you a joyful celebration of Juneteenth, Karen P.S. Juneteenth falling on Father’s Day adds an extra level of reflection to such a special day. Read my thoughts on it . To all who are celebrating today — both liberation and the ones we love — happy Father’s Day! name: Team Beto
subject: One major way we can continue this momentum…
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Beto for Texas
body:
Abbott is plummeting in the polls.
#
Here’s the deal:
The results in November could come down to just 1 or 2 percent. A new poll shows that _Abbott's support has plummeted_ since Beto entered the race last winter, and this is now a single digit race.
**We can continue this momentum by running ads to share Beto’s vision with millions of Texas voters.** But producing and running TV ads is really expensive in a state as big as Texas, and we need the financial resources to get them on air.
**[Please, will you make a $3 contribution right now to help us create and launch Beto’s new ads? We have the momentum in this race and need your support to keep it going.](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=bft-em-20220619-2.8fK_Jz&refcode=ema20220619-194688&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=194688%2E444211%2E3-XX4u)**
**[Contribute $3](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=bft-em-20220619-2.8fK_Jz&refcode=ema20220619-194688&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=20&akid=194688%2E444211%2E3-XX4u)**
Abbott has already spent _$4 million_ on ads earlier this year and is planning to spend over _$100 million_ before Election Day. We need the resources to fight back and to spread Beto’s positive vision for Texas across all 254 counties.
There is so much on the line in November: From commonsense gun reform to protecting a woman’s freedom to make her own decisions about her own body. Beto will unite Texans and bring together Democrats, Republicans, and Independents alike so we can move our state forward.
This is a crucial moment for our campaign. And to continue this momentum, we need millions of voters to hear Beto’s positive vision for Texas.
**[Make a $3 contribution right now to help us launch Beto’s ads, so we can reach the voters who will decide this election.](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=bft-em-20220619-2.8fK_Jz&refcode=ema20220619-194688&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=21&akid=194688%2E444211%2E3-XX4u)**
Thank you for helping us spread Beto’s positive vision for Texas.
More soon,
Team Beto

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bft-em-footer?refcode=em20220619-194688-footer&t=22&refcode2=194688_444211_3-XX4u&akid=194688%2E444211%2E3-XX4u)**

name: Kina Collins
subject: Happy Juneteenth!
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY KINA COLLINS FOR CONGRESS** ](https://click
body:
Today we celebrate the rich history and innumerable contributions that our people — BLACK PEOPLE — have made to this country. | | | [](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953yOQozAy4249GfohatM8XLCP7Gyf6bzrkjN8sXh_JjXvDm21fvmGLhqUe-quR3_x0a4ZLzkfrFqdtVKuNIuwQsn0c91jgEdSA5OANsq926BffC7faAQGaBY74u73WFJVk5_7dm8Ty4REYwj7zNXQFJKilVm8NsWhwoM7ffQtLDTmfx4BRk6E209KwwE-lyMO6IvfSGBazHUUY4OmstNatTfU-hQYs0eMUTjlrJpwMiJmYgfevE-A6nQeE3EswDcEPwBcsOBNNWIit763pM0OcWE5MRvZjO0MaVuajc8_EMguA/3n1/wgba41tuQ9aPbAJm_hLBYA/h0/JdUKua3S9uEQXItu1CtMpyYCJWbpUhzYInD1dB-eB48) Dear Friends, Happy Juneteenth y’all! Today we celebrate the rich history and innumerable contributions that our people — BLACK PEOPLE — have made to this country. As a young Black woman in America, I’ve grown up watching our government fail to deliver on its centuries’ old promise of freedom, justice and equality for our people. Our history is a history of Black survival **DESPITE** centuries of legalized oppression. It is a history of black excellence **DESPITE** institutionalized racism. **With every challenge thrown our way, we’ve fought back and we will never stop fighting until that promise for freedom, justice and equality is delivered.** The lives of Black Americans in Illinois’ 7th Congressional District are like the lives of so many Black Americans across this country. Defunded public schools. Food deserts. Rampant inequality. I’m fighting so that we can raise children in communities with clean air & water, grocery stores, quality education, free and accessible healthcare, and union jobs. It’s the future I’m fighting for because it’s the future every single one of us in this country deserves. For freedom, justice and equality for all our people. In Solidarity, Kina Collins

I recently visited my friend and fellow conservative, Jeremy Hunt, in GA-02. ** ** After spending time with him, I can assure you Jeremy Hunt is who Georgians need in office. ** ** Here are the facts:
* Jeremy Hunt’s district hasn’t gone RED in 150 years.

As we work together to fight for Wyoming and the issues that matter to us, there are some things you can count on: when Liz knows something is wrong, she will say so. She won’t waver or back down. clean_body: Surrender to pressure or intimidation | | As we work together to fight for Wyoming and the issues that matter to us, there are some things you can count on: when Liz knows something is wrong, she will say so. She won’t waver or back down. She won’t surrender to pressure or intimidation. She knows where to draw the line, and she knows that some things aren’t for sale. That’s the Code of the West and that’s what Wyoming voters deserve and expect. Truth. Resolve. Duty. Integrity. Honor. Paid for by Liz Cheney for Wyoming

name: Charles Booker
subject: Juneteenth
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Booker for Kentucky
body:
[1]Charles Booker

Peter,

Today we commemorate June 19, 1865, the day when Union soldiers arrived in
Texas and announced that all enslaved people were free, more than two
years after the Emancipation Proclamation was declared.

It’s a day of celebration for the liberation of Black Americans. But
Juneteenth is not simply a celebration to me. It is a heavy day as well.
It’s heavy because of the truth it exposes, and because the freedom we
were promised is still not fully in reach.

It’s clear that our country is still struggling to address its history of
systemic racism. From the recent massacre in Buffalo, to the cycle of
police violence that’s taken away the lives of our neighbors like Breonna
Taylor — there are constant reminders of the challenges we face. We’re
still struggling to even get our leaders to agree that heinous acts like
lynching should be a crime.

I don’t say all of this to paint a cynical picture. I say this because I
want to share with you why I’m hopeful for the future.

I’ve spent countless days standing for justice with my neighbors in
Kentucky. I stood next to my brothers and sisters in Louisville as we were
marching for Breonna Taylor, David McAtee, George Floyd, and so many other
Black Americans who have been killed by the agencies that we pay to
protect us.

There were moments it felt like our leaders couldn’t care less — that
change was out of reach.

But in the face of so much pain, we kept pushing. We kept showing up. We
kept building community. We kept standing for justice. We kept saying
Breonna Taylor’s name.

I’ve witnessed Kentuckians from all walks of life come together, day after
day, to organize. Together, we demanded change to save Black lives and
made sure that the City of Louisville passed Breonna’s Law and banned
no-knock warrants.

You are the reason why I know change is possible in Kentucky.

Recently, I made a video about the family members I’ve lost to lynching in
response to Rand’s vote to block lynching from being a federal hate
crime.

A lot of folks told me this video would be controversial. Too jarring.
Especially in a “red state” like Kentucky.

Instead we received an overwhelming response of love and support. It was
proof of the support we have in building our movement. And I can’t wait to
keep proving everyone wrong who claims that Kentucky isn’t ready for
change.

I believe in us. Because ultimately, we’re fighting because we just want
to be okay, and we just want to take care of our families. That’s what all
of this is about, and it’s why we’re pulling on the long, moral arc of the
universe towards justice.

Thank you,

Charles

http://act.charlesbooker.org/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=2092%2E346206%2Elny5T8

Contributions to Booker for Kentucky are not deductible for federal income tax purposes.
